They are kept in this separate, loadable\n11\t * module (rather than inline in index.html) so the render contract can be\n12\t * verified headlessly — tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js and\n13\t * tests/e2e/canvas_builtin_runner.js load this exact file under a minimal DOM\n14\t * shim and assert the rendered HTML, with no browser or network required.\n15\t *\n16\t * ESCAPING CONTRACT (the render-path escaping contract, see plan UI-Regen Agent\n17\t * \"Escaping contract\"): every dynamic value — registry descriptions, utterance\n18\t * text, per-source names, error detail, elapsed strings, LLM/worker-authored\n19\t * free text — is interpolated through escapeHtml(), which sets it via a text\n20\t * node (textContent → the browser's own escaper). No dynamic value is ever\n21\t * spliced raw into innerHTML. The built-in builders below all go through the\n22\t * same escapeHtml() the topic card uses, so they are bound by the identical\n23\t * escaping contract.\n24\t *\n25\t * In the browser, index.html loads this via <script src=\"/canvas.js\"> before\n26\t * the inline app script, so these declarations are globals just like they were\n27\t * when they lived inline. In Node, the trailing export shim exposes them via\n28\t * module.exports for the DOM tests.\n29\t */\n30\t\n31\t// Node.js compatibility: ensure document is available in Node for headless tests\n32\t// When loaded in Node (module.exports exists), make the global document accessible\n33\t// to the el() helper function which uses document.createElement() and .createTextNode()\n34\tif (typeof module !== 'undefined' && module.exports && typeof document === 'undefined') {\n35\t    var document = global.document;\n36\t}\n37\t\n38\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n39\t// Tunables (exported so tests can assert against the same constants)\n40\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n41\t\n42\t// Hot-path intents flag at 30s pending (their budget is 3s) — see plan, The\n43\t// Async Path → Visible aging + Degraded-State UX aged-pending row. This flag is\n44\t// applied PURELY client-side from the local placeholder's creation time so it\n45\t// survives a hung/wedged server (no SSE dependency).\n46\tvar PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS = 30000;\n47\t\n48\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n49\t// Existing topic-card render helpers\n50\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n51\t\n52\tfunction formatStaleness(seconds) {\n53\t    if (seconds < 60) return 'just now';\n54\t    if (seconds < 3600) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 60)}m ago`;\n55\t    if (seconds < 86400) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 3600)}h ago`;\n56\t    return `${Math.floor(seconds / 86400)}d ago`;\n57\t}\n58\t\n59\tfunction getStalenessLevel(seconds) {\n60\t    if (seconds < 600) return 'fresh';      // 10 minutes\n61\t    if (seconds < 3600) return 'stale';     // 1 hour\n62\t    return 'very-stale';\n63\t}\n64\t\n65\t/**\n66\t * Build a topic card from topic data.\n67\t *\n68\t * @param {TopicCardData} cardData - Topic card data structure\n69\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .topic-card element (or fallback-card element if card_fallback is set)\n70\t */\n71\tfunction createTopicCard(cardData) {\n72\t    const topic = cardData.topic;\n73\t    const staleness = cardData.staleness;\n74\t    const latestResult = cardData.latest_result;\n75\t\n76\t    // When the result has card_fallback=True, render the built-in generic fallback card\n77\t    // instead of the standard topic card. The fallback card is a complete card showing\n78\t    // result.data as a key/value grid (plan: Component Library → \"Built-in generic\n79\t    // fallback card\"). Topic metadata is preserved via data attributes for staleness\n80\t    // tracking and querying, but visually the card is the fallback presentation.\n81\t    if (latestResult && latestResult.card_fallback) {\n82\t        const fallbackCard = createFallbackCard(latestResult);\n83\t        // Preserve topic metadata for staleness tracking and querying\n84\t        fallbackCard.dataset.topicId = topic.id;\n85\t        fallbackCard.dataset.topicType = topic.type || 'adhoc';\n86\t        fallbackCard.dataset.cardFallback = '1';\n87\t        return fallbackCard;\n88\t    }\n89\t\n90\t    const card = document.createElement('div');\n91\t    card.className = 'topic-card';\n92\t    card.dataset.topicId = topic.id;\n93\t\n94\t    const stalenessLevel = getStalenessLevel(staleness.seconds);\n95\t    card.classList.add(stalenessLevel);\n96\t\n97\t    const typeClass = topic.type || 'adhoc';\n98\t\n99\t    // Expose the topic type as a data attribute alongside data-topic-id so cards\n100\t    // are queryable by type with a robust, stable selector (e.g.\n101\t    // [data-topic-type=\"research\"]) rather than relying on class names. The DOM\n102\t    // verification suites assert both dataset attributes are present per card.\n103\t    card.dataset.topicType = typeClass;\n104\t\n105\t    let html = `\n106\t        <div class=\"topic-header\">\n107\t            <div class=\"topic-label\">${escapeHtml(topic.label)}</div>\n108\t            <div>\n109\t                <span class=\"topic-type ${typeClass}\">${escapeHtml(topic.type || 'adhoc')}</span>\n110\t                ${stalenessLevel !== 'fresh' ? `<span class=\"stale-badge ${stalenessLevel}\">STALE</span>` : ''}\n111\t            </div>\n112\t        </div>\n113\t    `;\n114\t\n115\t    // Add latest result if available\n116\t    if (latestResult) {\n117\t        const urgencyClass = latestResult.urgency || 'normal';\n118\t        html += `\n119\t            <div class=\"result-content\">\n120\t                <div class=\"result-summary\">\n121\t                    ${escapeHtml(latestResult.summary)}\n122\t                    <span class=\"urgency-badge ${urgencyClass}\">${escapeHtml(urgencyClass)}</span>\n123\t                </div>\n124\t        `;\n125\t\n126\t        if (latestResult.data) {\n127\t            html += `<div class=\"result-data\">${escapeHtml(JSON.stringify(latestResult.data, null, 2))}</div>`;\n128\t        }\n129\t\n130\t        html += `</div>`;\n131\t    }\n132\t\n133\t    // Add staleness indicator\n134\t    const timeAgo = formatStaleness(staleness.seconds);\n135\t    html += `\n136\t        <div class=\"staleness-indicator ${stalenessLevel}\">\n137\t            <span class=\"staleness-dot ${stalenessLevel}\"></span>\n138\t            <span>Updated ${timeAgo}</span>\n139\t        </div>\n140\t    `;\n141\t\n142\t    card.innerHTML = html;\n143\t    return card;\n144\t}\n145\t\n146\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n147\t// Shared escaping + small DOM helpers (text-node based — escaping contract)\n148\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n149\t\n150\tfunction escapeHtml(text) {\n151\t    const div = document.createElement('div');\n152\t    div.textContent = text == null ? '' : String(text);\n153\t    return div.innerHTML;\n154\t}\n155\t\n156\t/**\n157\t * Build a DOM element with tag + className + a list of children/strings.\n158\t *\n159\t * Strings are appended as TEXT NODES (never spliced into innerHTML), which is\n160\t * the escaping contract's literal form — a markup-looking log line renders as\n161\t * literal text instead of breaking layout. Use this for any value that must be\n162\t * inserted verbatim as text.\n163\t */\n164\tfunction el(tag, className, children) {\n165\t    const node = document.createElement(tag);\n166\t    if (className) node.className = className;\n167\t    (children || []).forEach(function (c) {\n168\t        if (c == null) return;\n169\t        if (typeof c === 'string' || typeof c === 'number') {\n170\t            node.appendChild(document.createTextNode(String(c)));\n171\t        } else {\n172\t            node.appendChild(c);\n173\t        }\n174\t    });\n175\t    return node;\n176\t}\n177\t\n178\t// Seconds → \"0s\" / \"12s\" / \"1m 05s\" elapsed label for pending cards.\n179\tfunction formatElapsed(ms) {\n180\t    const s = Math.max(0, Math.floor((ms || 0) / 1000));\n181\t    if (s < 60) return s + 's';\n182\t    const m = Math.floor(s / 60);\n183\t    const rem = s % 60;\n184\t    return m + 'm ' + (rem < 10 ? '0' : '') + rem + 's';\n185\t}\n186\t\n187\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n188\t// (2) First-run welcome card — built-in, zero DB dependence\n189\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n190\t\n191\t// Default example utterances used when the registry yields no project with a\n192\t// supported intent to derive one from. These never touch the component DB.\n193\tvar DEFAULT_EXAMPLE_UTTERANCES = [\n194\t    'Has the options pipeline caught up?',\n195\t    'What is the state of the ibkr mcp?',\n196\t    'Queue up a research task on recent pipeline errors — no rush.'\n197\t];\n198\t\n199\t// Map a supported intent type to an example utterance fragment, so the welcome\n200\t// card's examples are drawn from the projects the router actually knows about\n201\t// (plan: Cold start & demo seed — \"2-3 example utterances drawn from those\n202\t// projects' supported intents\").\n203\tvar INTENT_EXAMPLES = {\n204\t    status: 'What is the status of {proj}?',\n205\t    action: 'Restart the {proj} deployment.',\n206\t    brainstorm: 'Brainstorm trade-offs for {proj}.',\n207\t    lookup: 'Pull up recent logs for {proj}.',\n208\t    'task-profile': 'Queue up a research task on {proj} — no rush.',\n209\t    'self-modification': 'Add an alias to the {proj} registry entry.',\n210\t    'monitoring-config': 'Watch {proj} for pod restarts.',\n211\t    reminder: 'Remind me to check {proj} in an hour.'\n212\t};\n213\t\n214\tfunction _deriveExamples(projects) {\n215\t    const out = [];\n216\t    const seen = {};\n217\t    // Prefer projects that carry a description (registry.yaml entries) so the\n218\t    // welcome examples reference real, described projects — not bare discovered\n219\t    // repos with auto-README blurbs.\n220\t    const ordered = (projects || []).slice().sort(function (a, b) {\n221\t        const ad = a && a.description ? 0 : 1;\n222\t        const bd = b && b.description ? 0 : 1;\n223\t        return ad - bd;\n224\t    });\n225\t    ordered.forEach(function (p) {\n226\t        if (out.length >= 3) return;\n227\t        (p.intent_support || []).forEach(function (intent) {\n228\t            if (out.length >= 3 || seen[intent]) return;\n229\t            const tmpl = INTENT_EXAMPLES[intent];\n230\t            if (!tmpl) return;\n231\t            seen[intent] = true;\n232\t            out.push(tmpl.replace('{proj}', p.slug || p.name || 'the project'));\n233\t        });\n234\t    });\n235\t    return out.length ? out : DEFAULT_EXAMPLE_UTTERANCES.slice(0, 3);\n236\t}\n237\t\n238\t// Cap the welcome-card project list so a 38-repo registry doesn't overwhelm the\n239\t// first frame. The first N are shown; an \"+N more\" note counts the rest.\n240\tvar WELCOME_PROJECT_CAP = 12;\n241\t\n242\t/**\n243\t * Build the first-run welcome card.\n244\t *\n245\t * @param {Array} projects  registry entries [{slug,name,description,intent_support,aliases}]\n246\t * @param {string} [description]  one-line description of aide-de-camp\n247\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .welcome-card element (data-builtin=\"welcome\")\n248\t *\n249\t * Renders even against an empty component library / DB — the project list comes\n250\t * from config/registry.yaml (served at /api/v1/registry), not the DB. The first\n251\t * real result replaces it (the canvas drops it once any topic card exists).\n252\t */\n253\tfunction createWelcomeCard(projects, description) {\n254\t    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card welcome-card');\n255\t    card.dataset.builtin = 'welcome';\n256\t\n257\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n258\t        el('span', 'builtin-icon', ['👋']),\n259\t        el('span', 'builtin-title', ['Welcome to ADC'])\n260\t    ]));\n261\t\n262\t    card.appendChild(el('p', 'builtin-desc', [\n263\t        description || 'A single input surface that routes what you say to the right project, fetches in parallel, and renders results as live cards.'\n264\t    ]));\n265\t\n266\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-section-title', ['Registered projects']));\n267\t    const list = el('ul', 'builtin-project-list');\n268\t    (projects || []).forEach(function (p) {\n269\t        const slug = p.slug || p.name || 'project';\n270\t        const desc = p.description || '';\n271\t        const intents = (p.intent_support || []).join(' · ');\n272\t        const li = el('li', 'builtin-project');\n273\t        li.appendChild(el('strong', 'builtin-project-slug', [slug]));\n274\t        if (desc) li.appendChild(document.createTextNode(' — ' + desc));\n275\t        if (intents) li.appendChild(el('span', 'builtin-intents', [' ' + intents]));\n276\t        list.appendChild(li);\n277\t    });\n278\t    if (!(projects && projects.length)) {\n279\t        list.appendChild(el('li', 'builtin-project', ['No projects registered yet.']));\n280\t    }\n281\t    card.appendChild(list);\n282\t\n283\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-section-title', ['Try asking']));\n284\t    const examples = el('ul', 'builtin-examples');\n285\t    _deriveExamples(projects).forEach(function (ex) {\n286\t        examples.appendChild(el('li', 'builtin-example', [ex]));\n287\t    });\n288\t    card.appendChild(examples);\n289\t\n290\t    return card;\n291\t}\n292\t\n293\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n294\t// Container decision — the headlessly-testable core of loadTopics()\n295\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n296\t\n297\t/**\n298\t * Decide what the canvas topic-container shows for a given card set.\n299\t *\n300\t * This is the shared render path index.html's ``loadTopics()`` calls after\n301\t * ``GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics``: on a fresh session with zero cards it\n302\t * shows the first-run welcome card (built from the registry project list — no\n303\t * DB/result dependence); the moment any real card exists it shows only topic\n304\t * cards, which drops any welcome card (never alongside real cards). The caller\n305\t * clears the container and appends the returned nodes.\n306\t *\n307\t * Kept here (not inline in index.html) so the zero→welcome / first-result→drop\n308\t * contract is verifiable headlessly: ``tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js`` drives\n309\t * this exact function (``--container`` mode) and asserts the welcome card\n310\t * renders from an empty card set and is absent once a real card is present.\n311\t *\n312\t * @param {Array} [cards]      card dicts from /topics (empty → welcome card)\n313\t * @param {Array} [projects]   registry entries for the welcome card\n314\t * @param {string} [description] one-line description for the welcome card\n315\t * @returns {HTMLElement[]}    child nodes for the container\n316\t */\n317\tfunction buildContainerChildren(cards, projects, description) {\n318\t    if (!cards || cards.length === 0) {\n319\t        return [createWelcomeCard(projects, description)];\n320\t    }\n321\t    return cards.map(function (c) { return createTopicCard(c); });\n322\t}\n323\t\n324\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n325\t// (3) Pending/ack cards — submit-time placeholder + per-thread pending\n326\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n327\t\n328\t/**\n329\t * Build a submit-time pending placeholder.\n330\t *\n331\t * Created LOCALLY at dispatch() submit time, BEFORE any server response, so a\n332\t * hung/wedged server still leaves a card on canvas to age (plan, Escalate\n333\t * Strand → Pending/ack card render path). It splits into per-thread pending\n334\t * cards when the dispatch ack (the /dispatch HTTP response) carries intent_ids.\n335\t *\n336\t * @param {string} utterance   the raw text being dispatched\n337\t * @param {number} createdAt   Date.now() at placeholder creation (ms epoch)\n338\t * @param {string} [pendingId] stable id for this placeholder (utterance id)\n339\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .pending-card.placeholder (data-builtin=\"pending\")\n340\t */\n341\tfunction createPendingPlaceholderCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId) {\n342\t    const card = _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId, 'Sending…');\n343\t    card.classList.add('placeholder');\n344\t    card.dataset.pendingKind = 'placeholder';\n345\t    return card;\n346\t}\n347\t\n348\t/**\n349\t * Build a per-thread pending card (one per dispatch-ack intent_id). Carries the\n350\t * per-source progress state ('3/5 sources in') and the elapsed-time footer.\n351\t *\n352\t * @param {string} intentId    the intent this thread tracks (result_created key)\n353\t * @param {string} utterance   the originating utterance fragment/text\n354\t * @param {number} createdAt   ms epoch inherited from the placeholder\n355\t * @param {object} [progress]  {completed, total} initial per-source progress\n356\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .pending-card.thread (data-builtin=\"pending\")\n357\t */\n358\tfunction createPendingThreadCard(intentId, utterance, createdAt, progress) {\n359\t    const card = _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, intentId, 'Working on it…');\n360\t    card.classList.add('thread');\n361\t    card.dataset.pendingKind = 'thread';\n362\t    if (progress) _setProgress(card, progress);\n363\t    return card;\n364\t}\n365\t\n366\t/**\n367\t * Split a submit-time placeholder into per-thread pending cards on the dispatch\n368\t * ack. The /dispatch ack response carries ``intent_ids`` — one per routed\n369\t * thread — so this produces one per-thread pending card per intent_id, each\n370\t * inheriting the placeholder's ``createdAt`` (so the 30s aged timer is\n371\t * continuous across the split — the timer started at submit, not at ack) and\n372\t * the originating utterance. (plan, Escalate Strand → Pending/ack card render\n373\t * path: \"splits into per-thread pending cards when the dispatch ack arrives\".)\n374\t *\n375\t * This is the headlessly-testable core of the split: index.html's dispatch()\n376\t * calls it with the ack's intent_ids and replaces the placeholder DOM node with\n377\t * the returned cards; the Node DOM shim exercises it directly (no browser) to\n378\t * prove the split is one-card-per-thread.\n379\t *\n380\t * @param {string} utterance   the originating utterance text\n381\t * @param {number} createdAt   ms epoch inherited from the placeholder\n382\t * @param {string[]} intentIds the ack's intent_ids (one per routed thread)\n383\t * @returns {HTMLElement[]} one .pending-card.thread per intent_id\n384\t */\n385\tfunction splitPlaceholderToThreads(utterance, createdAt, intentIds) {\n386\t    return (intentIds || []).map(function (id) {\n387\t        return createPendingThreadCard(id, utterance, createdAt);\n388\t    });\n389\t}\n390\t\n391\t// Shared skeleton for both placeholder + per-thread pending cards.\n392\tfunction _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId, titleText) {\n393\t    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card pending-card');\n394\t    card.dataset.builtin = 'pending';\n395\t    card.dataset.createdAt = String(createdAt || 0);\n396\t    if (pendingId) card.dataset.pendingId = String(pendingId);\n397\t\n398\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n399\t        el('span', 'pending-spinner'),\n400\t        el('span', 'builtin-title pending-title', [titleText || 'Pending…'])\n401\t    ]));\n402\t\n403\t    if (utterance) {\n404\t        card.appendChild(el('p', 'pending-utterance', [utterance]));\n405\t    }\n406\t\n407\t    const progress = el('div', 'pending-progress');\n408\t    progress.style.display = 'none';\n409\t    card.appendChild(progress);\n410\t\n411\t    const elapsed = el('div', 'pending-elapsed', [\n412\t        formatElapsed(0) + ' elapsed'\n413\t    ]);\n414\t    card.appendChild(elapsed);\n415\t\n416\t    // Aged note (hidden until the 30s threshold) — see applyAgedTreatment().\n417\t    const agedNote = el('div', 'pending-aged-note');\n418\t    agedNote.style.display = 'none';\n419\t    agedNote.appendChild(document.createTextNode('Taking longer than expected — the server may be hung. '));\n420\t    const retry = el('button', 'pending-retry', ['Retry']);\n421\t    agedNote.appendChild(retry);\n422\t    card.appendChild(agedNote);\n423\t\n424\t    return card;\n425\t}\n426\t\n427\tfunction _setProgress(card, progress) {\n428\t    const node = card.querySelector('.pending-progress');\n429\t    if (!node || !progress) return;\n430\t    const completed = Math.max(0, parseInt(progress.completed, 10) || 0);\n431\t    const total = Math.max(0, parseInt(progress.total, 10) || 0);\n432\t    node.textContent = completed + '/' + total + ' sources in';\n433\t    node.dataset.completed = String(completed);\n434\t    node.dataset.total = String(total);\n435\t    node.style.display = total > 0 ? '' : 'none';\n436\t}\n437\t\n438\t/**\n439\t * Recompute the elapsed-time footer for a pending card against `now` (ms).\n440\t * Returns the elapsed ms. Pure: no timers, no globals beyond the card's own\n441\t * data-createdAt — so the headless harness can drive it via a mock clock.\n442\t */\n443\tfunction tickPendingElapsed(card, now) {\n444\t    const createdAt = parseInt(card.dataset.createdAt, 10) || 0;\n445\t    const elapsedMs = Math.max(0, (now || 0) - createdAt);\n446\t    const node = card.querySelector('.pending-elapsed');\n447\t    if (node) node.textContent = formatElapsed(elapsedMs) + ' elapsed';\n448\t    return elapsedMs;\n449\t}\n450\t\n451\t/**\n452\t * Apply (or remove) the aged-pending treatment based on elapsed ms vs the 30s\n453\t * threshold. Pure client-side — survives a hung server (no SSE dependency).\n454\t * Returns true if the card is currently flagged aged.\n455\t */\n456\tfunction applyAgedTreatment(card, now) {\n457\t    const elapsedMs = tickPendingElapsed(card, now);\n458\t    const aged = elapsedMs >= PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS;\n459\t    card.classList.toggle('aged', aged);\n460\t    const note = card.querySelector('.pending-aged-note');\n461\t    if (note) note.style.display = aged ? '' : 'none';\n462\t    return aged;\n463\t}\n464\t\n465\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n466\t// (4) Error / clarification family — Degraded-State UX, filled client-side\n467\t//     from SSE error events\n468\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n469\t\n470\t// Display metadata per error variant. title + icon + a one-line description\n471\t// template. Variants accept both snake_case and kebab-case keys (normalized in\n472\t// _errorVariant()). Maps directly to the Degraded-State UX matrix rows.\n473\tvar ERROR_VARIANTS = {\n474\t    router_unavailable: {\n475\t        title: 'Router unavailable',\n476\t        icon: '🔌',\n477\t        detail: 'The LLM proxy (ZAI) was unreachable at the router stage, so this utterance could not be routed.'\n478\t    },\n479\t    all_sources_failed: {\n480\t        title: 'No data available',\n481\t        icon: '📭',\n482\t        detail: 'Every fetch source failed or timed out, so there is nothing to synthesize.'\n483\t    },\n484\t    synthesis_failed: {\n485\t        title: 'Summary unavailable',\n486\t        icon: '⚠️',\n487\t        detail: 'The raw data was fetched, but the summary could not be produced.'\n488\t    },\n489\t    malformed_router_output: {\n490\t        title: \"Couldn't parse that into intents\",\n491\t        icon: '🧩',\n492\t        detail: 'The router returned output the system could not interpret.'\n493\t    },\n494\t    no_match: {\n495\t        title: 'No matching project',\n496\t        icon: '❓',\n497\t        detail: 'No registered project matched this request.'\n498\t    }\n499\t};\n500\t\n501\tfunction _normalizeVariant(errorType) {\n502\t    if (!errorType) return 'no_match';\n503\t    const key = String(errorType).trim().replace(/-/g, '_').toLowerCase();\n504\t    return ERROR_VARIANTS[key] ? key : 'no_match';\n505\t}\n506\t\n507\t/**\n508\t * Build an error/clarification card from an SSE error event payload.\n509\t *\n510\t * @param {ErrorCardData} data - Error card data structure\n511\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .error-card element (data-builtin=\"error\")\n512\t *\n513\t * Per-source failure lists, the echoed utterance, raw degraded data, and any\n514\t * detail text are all inserted as text nodes (escaping contract).\n515\t */\n516\tfunction createErrorCard(data) {\n517\t    data = data || {};\n518\t    const variant = _normalizeVariant(data.error_type);\n519\t    const meta = ERROR_VARIANTS[variant];\n520\t\n521\t    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card error-card error-' + variant);\n522\t    card.dataset.builtin = 'error';\n523\t    card.dataset.errorType = variant;\n524\t    if (data.intent_id) card.dataset.intentId = String(data.intent_id);\n525\t    if (data.pending_id) card.dataset.pendingId = String(data.pending_id);\n526\t\n527\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n528\t        el('span', 'builtin-icon', [meta.icon]),\n529\t        el('span', 'builtin-title', [meta.title])\n530\t    ]));\n531\t\n532\t    const detail = data.detail || meta.detail;\n533\t    card.appendChild(el('p', 'error-detail', [detail]));\n534\t\n535\t    // Echo the utterance so nothing is ever lost (router_unavailable /\n536\t    // malformed_router_output rows). Rendered as text, never raw HTML.\n537\t    if (data.utterance) {\n538\t        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-utterance-wrap', [\n539\t            el('span', 'error-utterance-label', ['You said: ']),\n540\t            el('span', 'error-utterance', ['“' + data.utterance + '”'])\n541\t        ]));\n542\t    }\n543\t\n544\t    // Per-source failure list (all_sources_failed row).\n545\t    const sources = data.sources;\n546\t    if (Array.isArray(sources) && sources.length) {\n547\t        const ul = el('ul', 'error-source-list');\n548\t        sources.forEach(function (s) {\n549\t            const name = (s && (s.name || s.source)) || 'source';\n550\t            const reason = (s && (s.reason || s.error)) || 'failed';\n551\t            ul.appendChild(el('li', 'error-source', [\n552\t                el('span', 'error-source-name', [name + ': ']),\n553\t                el('span', 'error-source-reason', [reason])\n554\t            ]));\n555\t        });\n556\t        card.appendChild(ul);\n557\t    }\n558\t\n559\t    // Degraded raw data (synthesis_failed row): fetched data is never discarded.\n560\t    if (data.data != null) {\n561\t        const raw = typeof data.data === 'string' ? data.data : JSON.stringify(data.data, null, 2);\n562\t        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-raw-label', ['Fetched data']));\n563\t        card.appendChild(el('pre', 'error-raw', [raw]));\n564\t    }\n565\t\n566\t    // No-match clarification: list the registered projects so the user can pick.\n567\t    if (variant === 'no_match' && Array.isArray(data.registered_projects) && data.registered_projects.length) {\n568\t        const ul = el('ul', 'error-project-list');\n569\t        data.registered_projects.forEach(function (p) {\n570\t            const slug = typeof p === 'string' ? p : (p.slug || p.name || 'project');\n571\t            ul.appendChild(el('li', 'error-project', [slug]));\n572\t        });\n573\t        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-projects-wrap', [\n574\t            el('span', 'error-projects-label', ['Registered projects: ']),\n575\t            ul\n576\t        ]));\n577\t    }\n578\t\n579\t    // Recovery action button. Variant-aware label.\n580\t    const retryLabel = (variant === 'malformed_router_output' || variant === 'no_match')\n581\t        ? 'Edit & resend'\n582\t        : 'Retry';\n583\t    card.appendChild(el('button', 'error-retry', [retryLabel]));\n584\t\n585\t    return card;\n586\t}\n587\t\n588\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n589\t// Canvas Card Type Definitions\n590\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n591\t\n592\t/**\n593\t * @typedef {Object} StuckCardData\n594\t * @property {string} bead_id - The bead reference ID that is stuck\n595\t * @property {string} stuck_reason - The reason why the bead is stuck (refusal reason)\n596\t * @property {number} [refusal_count] - Number of refusals that caused the stuck state\n597\t * @property {string} [message] - Optional message describing the stuck state\n598\t * @property {string} [action_hint] - Optional hint for user action to resolve\n599\t */\n600\t\n601\t/**\n602\t * @typedef {Object} FailedCardData\n603\t * @property {string} bead_id - The bead reference ID that failed\n604\t * @property {string} failure_reason - The reason why the task failed\n605\t * @property {string} [error_type] - Optional error type/category\n606\t * @property {string} [message] - Optional message describing the failure\n607\t */\n608\t\n609\t/**\n610\t * @typedef {Object} ErrorCardData\n611\t * @property {string} error_type - The error variant type\n612\t * @property {string} [utterance] - The original user utterance\n613\t * @property {string} [detail] - Error detail message\n614\t * @property {Array<{name?: string, source?: string, reason?: string, error?: string}>} [sources] - Failed sources list\n615\t * @property {*} [data] - Raw degraded data\n616\t * @property {string} [intent_id] - Associated intent ID\n617\t * @property {string} [pending_id] - Associated pending ID\n618\t * @property {Array<string|{slug?: string, name?: string}>} [registered_projects] - Projects for no-match variant\n619\t */\n620\t\n621\t/**\n622\t * @typedef {Object} PendingCardData\n623\t * @property {string} utterance - The utterance being processed\n624\t * @property {number} createdAt - Creation timestamp (ms epoch)\n625\t * @property {string} [pendingId] - Stable ID for this placeholder\n626\t * @property {number} [completed] - Number of completed sources (for progress)\n627\t * @property {number} [total] - Total number of sources (for progress)\n628\t */\n629\t\n630\t/**\n631\t * @typedef {Object} TopicCardData\n632\t * @property {Object} topic - The topic object\n633\t * @property {string} topic.id - Topic ID\n634\t * @property {string} topic.label - Topic label\n635\t * @property {string} [topic.type] - Topic type (project, research, personal, exception, compound)\n636\t * @property {Object} staleness - Staleness info\n637\t * @property {number} staleness.seconds - Seconds since last update\n638\t * @property {Object} [latest_result] - Optional latest result\n639\t * @property {string} latest_result.summary - Result summary\n640\t * @property {string} [latest_result.urgency] - Result urgency (critical, high, normal, low)\n641\t * @property {*} [latest_result.data] - Result data\n642\t * @property {boolean} [latest_result.card_fallback] - True when no component matched (fallback card rendered)\n643\t */\n644\t\n645\t/**\n646\t * @typedef {Object} FallbackCardData\n647\t * @property {string} [summary] - Result summary\n648\t * @property {*} [data] - Result data\n649\t * @property {string} [urgency] - Result urgency\n650\t */\n651\t\n652\t/**\n653\t * @typedef {StuckCardData | FailedCardData | ErrorCardData | PendingCardData | TopicCardData | FallbackCardData} CardData\n654\t * Union type for all card data structures\n655\t */\n656\t\n657\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n658\t// (5) Stuck/Failed cards — circuit breaker and terminal failure cards\n659\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n660\t\n661\t/**\n662\t * Build a stuck card for a fenced bead.\n663\t *\n664\t * Plan §10 The Async Path: displayed when a bead is fenced due to repeated\n665\t * refusals or timeout. Shows the bead reference, refusal count, and latest reason.\n666\t *\n667\t * @param {StuckCardData} data - Stuck card data structure\n668\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .stuck-card element (data-builtin=\"stuck\")\n669\t */\n670\tfunction createStuckCard(data) {\n671\t    data = data || {};\n672\t    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card stuck-card');\n673\t    card.dataset.builtin = 'stuck';\n674\t    if (data.bead_id) card.dataset.beadId = String(data.bead_id);\n675\t\n676\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n677\t        el('span', 'builtin-icon', ['🚧']),\n678\t attrs: dict[str, str] | None = None) -> list[\"_Node\"]:\n51\t        \"\"\"All descendants matching tag / class / exact attribute values.\"\"\"\n52\t        out: list[_Node] = []\n53\t        for      schema = f.read()\n88\t            conn = self._get_conn()\n89\t            conn.executescript(schema)\n90\t            conn.commit()\n91\t\n92\t    def create_component(\n93\t        self,\n94\t        component_id = f\"comp-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}\"\n113\t        created_at = int(time.time())\n114\t\n115\t        conn = self._get_conn()\n116\t\n117\t        # Create component\n118\t           \"\"\",\n132\t            (component_id, html_template, created_at, change_note)\n133\t        )\n134\t\n135\t        conn.commit()\n136\t\n137\t        return Component(\n138\t   \"\"\"Get a component by ID.\"\"\"\n150\t        conn = self._get_conn()\n151\t        row = conn.execute(\n152\t            \"SELECT id, name, description, html_template, version, created_at, last_used, usage_count \"\n153\t            \"FROM components WHERE id = ?\",\n154\t            (component_id,)\n155\t        ).fetchone()\n156\t\n157\t        if row:\n158\t            return version=row[4],\n164\t                created_at=row[5],\n165\t                last_used=row[6],\n166\t                usage_count=row[7]\n167\t            )\n168\t        return None\n169\t\n170\t    def find_best_component(\n171\t        self,\n172\t        result_type: str,\n173\t        result_data: Dict[str, Any]\n174\t    ) -> Optional[Component]:\n175\t        \"\"\"\n176\t        Find the best-fit component for a result.\n177\t\n178\t        Uses semantic matching on component descriptions and\n179\t        historical usage patterns.\n180\t\n181\t        Args:\n182\t            result_type: The type of result (e.g., \"pod-status\")\n183\t            result_data: The structured result data\n184\t\n185\t        Returns:\n186\t            The best matching Component, or None if no good match\n187\t        \"\"\"\n188\t        conn = self._get_conn()\n189\t\n190\t        # First, check usage patterns for this exact result type\n191\t        pattern_row = conn.execute(\n192\t            \"\"\"\n193\t            SELECT component_id, match_score\n194\t            FROM component_usage_patterns\n195\t            WHERE result_type = ?\n196\t            ORDER BY match_score DESC, sample_count DESC\n197\t            LIMIT 1\n198\t            \"\"\",\n199\t            (result_type,)\n200\t        ).fetchone()\n201\t\n202\t        if pattern_row and pattern_row[1] >= 0.7:  # 70% confidence threshold\n203\t            component = self.get_component(pattern_row[0])\n204\t            if component:\n205\t                return component\n206\t\n207\t        # Fallback: semantic search on component descriptions\n208\t        # (In production, this would use embeddings. For now, use simple keyword match)\n209\t        components = conn.execute(\n210\t            \"SELECT id, name, description FROM components WHERE usage_count > 0\"\n211\t        ).fetchall()\n212\t\n213\t        best_component = None\n214\t        best_score = 0.0\n215\t\n216\t        for comp_id, name, description in components:\n217\t            score = self._semantic_score(result_type, name, description)\n218\t            if score > best_score:\n219\t                best_score = score\n220\t                best_component = self.get_component(comp_id)\n221\t\n222\t        # Only return if we have reasonable confidence\n223\t        if best_score >= 0.5:\n224\t            return best_component\n225\t\n226\t        return None\n227\t\n228\t    def select_component_for_result_type(\n229\t        self,\n230\t        result_type: str,\n231\t        match_threshold: float = 0.7,\n232\t    ) -> Optional[Component]:\n233\t        \"\"\"Deterministic hot-path selection — the dispatch card selector.\n234\t\n235\t        Returns the highest-``match_score`` component mapped to ``result_type``\n236\t        in ``component_usage_patterns`` whose score clears ``match_threshold``,\n237\t        or None. No LLM, no semantic fallback, no generation (plan: The Hot Path\n238\t        / UI-Regen Agent — \"highest match_score in component_usage_patterns for\n239\t        the result_type, no LLM call\"). A None return is the signal that the\n240\t        result must fall to the built-in generic fallback card.\n241\t\n242\t        Distinct from :meth:`find_best_component`, which the *async* UI-regen\n243\t        agent uses to steward the library (it may fall back to semantic search\n244\t        and ultimately generate a new component). The hot path must never do\n245\t        either — it only reads the recorded mappings.\n246\t        \"\"\"\n247\t        conn = self._get_conn()\n248\t        pattern_row = conn.execute(\n249\t            \"\"\"\n250\t            SELECT component_id, match_score\n251\t            FROM component_usage_patterns\n252\t            WHERE result_type = ? AND match_score >= ?\n253\t            ORDER BY match_score DESC, sample_count DESC\n254\t            LIMIT 1\n255\t            \"\"\",\n256\t            (result_type, match_threshold),\n257\t        ).fetchone()\n258\t\n259\t        if not pattern_row:\n260\t            return None\n261\t\n262\t        return self.get_component(pattern_row[0])\n263\t\n264\t    def _semantic_score(self, result_type: str, comp_name: str, comp_description: str) -> float:\n265\t        \"\"\"\n266\t        Calculate semantic match score.\n267\t\n268\t        Simple keyword-based scoring. In production, use embeddings.\n269\t        \"\"\"\n270\t        result_type_lower = result_type.lower()\n271\t        comp_name_lower = comp_name.lower()\n272\t        comp_desc_lower = comp_description.lower()\n273\t\n274\t        score = 0.0\n275\t\n276\t        # Exact name match\n277\t        if result_type_lower == comp_name_lower:\n278\t            return 1.0\n279\t\n280\t        # Name contains result type\n281\t        if result_type_lower in comp_name_lower:\n282\t            score += 0.7\n283\t\n284\t        # Description contains keywords from result type\n285\t        result_keywords = result_type_lower.replace('-', ' ').split()\n286\t        for keyword in result_keywords:\n287\t            if keyword in comp_desc_lower:\n288\t                score += 0.1\n289\t\n290\t        return min(score, 1.0)\n291\t\n292\t    def update_component(\n293\t        self,\n294\t        component_id: str,\n295\t        html_template: str,\n296\t        change_note: str\n297\t    ) -> Optional[Component]:\n298\t        \"\"\"\n299\t        Update a component to a new version.\n300\t\n301\t        Invalidates all cached cards using this component.\n302\t\n303\t        Args:\n304\t            component_id: The component to update\n305\t            html_template: The new template\n306\t            change_note: Why this change was made\n307\t\n308\t        Returns:\n309\t            The updated Component, or None if not found\n310\t        \"\"\"\n311\t        conn = self._get_conn()\n312\t\n313\t        # Get current version\n314\t        row = conn.execute(\n315\t            \"SELECT version FROM components WHERE id = ?\",\n316\t            (component_id,)\n317\t        ).fetchone()\n318\t\n319\t        if not row:\n320\t            return None\n321\t\n322\t        new_version = row[0] + 1\n323\t        now = int(time.time())\n324\t\n325\t        # Update component\n326\t        conn.execute(\n327\t            \"\"\"\n328\t            UPDATE components\n329\t            SET html_template = ?, version = ?, last_used = ?\n330\t            WHERE id = ?\n331\t            \"\"\",\n332\t            (html_template, new_version, now, component_id)\n333\t        )\n334\t\n335\t        # Add version history\n336\t        conn.execute(\n337\t            \"\"\"\n338\t            INSERT INTO component_versions (component_id, version, html_template, created_at, change_note)\n339\t            VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\n340\t            \"\"\",\n341\t            (component_id, new_version, html_template, now, change_note)\n342\t        )\n343\t\n344\t        # Invalidate cached cards\n345\t        conn.execute(\n346\t            \"DELETE FROM card_cache WHERE component_id = ?\",\n347\t            (component_id,)\n348\t        )\n349\t\n350\t        conn.commit()\n351\t\n352\t        return self.get_component(component_id)\n353\t\n354\t    def cache_card(\n355\t        self,\n356\t        result_id: str,\n357\t        component_id: str,\n358\t        component_version: int,\n359\t        layout_bucket: str,\n360\t        rendered_html: str\n361\t    ):\n362\t        \"\"\"\n363\t        Cache a rendered card.\n364\t\n365\t        Args:\n366\t            result_id: The result this card renders\n367\t            component_id: The component used\n368\t            component_version: The component version\n369\t            layout_bucket: The layout bucket\n370\t            rendered_html: The rendered HTML\n371\t        \"\"\"\n372\t        if layout_bucket not in self.LAYOUT_BUCKETS:\n373\t            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid layout bucket: {layout_bucket}\")\n374\t\n375\t        conn = self._get_conn()\n376\t        conn.execute(\n377\t            \"\"\"\n378\t            INSERT OR REPLACE INTO card_cache\n379\t            (result_id, component_id, component_version, layout_bucket, rendered_html, created_at)\n380\t            VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\n381\t            \"\"\",\n382\t            (result_id, component_id, component_version, layout_bucket, rendered_html, int(time.time()))\n383\t        )\n384\t        conn.commit()\n385\t\n386\t        # Update component usage stats\n387\t        conn.execute(\n388\t            \"\"\"\n389\t            UPDATE components\n390\t            SET usage_count = usage_count + 1, last_used = ?\n391\t            WHERE id = ?\n392\t            \"\"\",\n393\t            (int(time.time()), component_id)\n394\t        )\n395\t        conn.commit()\n396\t\n397\t    def get_cached_card(\n398\t        self,\n399\t        result_id: str,\n400\t        component_id: str,\n401\t        layout_bucket: str\n402\t    ) -> Optional[CachedCard]:\n403\t        \"\"\"Get a cached card if it exists and is up-to-date.\"\"\"\n404\t        conn = self._get_conn()\n405\t        row = conn.execute(\n406\t            \"\"\"\n407\t            SELECT result_id, component_id, component_version, layout_bucket, rendered_html, created_at\n408\t            FROM card_cache\n409\t            WHERE result_id = ? AND component_id = ? AND layout_bucket = ?\n410\t            \"\"\",\n411\t            (result_id, component_id, layout_bucket)\n412\t        ).fetchone()\n413\t\n414\t        if row:\n415\t            return CachedCard(\n416\t                result_id=row[0],\n417\t                component_id=row[1],\n418\t                component_version=row[2],\n419\t                layout_bucket=row[3],\n420\t                rendered_html=row[4],\n421\t                created_at=row[5]\n422\t            )\n423\t        return None\n424\t\n425\t    def invalidate_result(self, result_id: str):\n426\t        \"\"\"Invalidate all cached cards for a result.\"\"\"\n427\t        conn = self._get_conn()\n428\t        conn.execute(\n429\t            \"DELETE FROM card_cache WHERE result_id = ?\",\n430\t            (result_id,)\n431\t        )\n432\t        conn.commit()\n433\t\n434\t    def record_usage_pattern(\n435\t        self,\n436\t        component_id: str,\n437\t        result_type: str,\n438\t        match_score: float\n439\t    ):\n440\t        \"\"\"\n441\t        Record a component usage pattern for future matching.\n442\t\n443\t        Args:\n444\t            component_id: The component that was used\n445\t            result_type: The type of result it rendered\n446\t            match_score: How well it matched (0-1)\n447\t        \"\"\"\n448\t        conn = self._get_conn()\n449\t        now = int(time.time())\n450\t\n451\t        conn.execute(\n452\t            \"\"\"\n453\t            INSERT INTO component_usage_patterns (component_id, result_type, match_score, sample_count, last_matched)\n454\t            VALUES (?, ?, ?, 1, ?)\n455\t            ON CONFLICT(component_id, result_type)\n456\t            DO UPDATE SET\n457\t                match_score = (match_score * sample_count + ?) / (sample_count + 1),\n458\t                sample_count = sample_count + 1,\n459\t                last_matched = ?\n460\t            \"\"\",\n461\t            (component_id, result_type, match_score, now, match_score, now)\n462\t        )\n463\t        conn.commit()\n464\t\n465\t    def list_components(self, limit: int = 50) -> List[Component]:\n466\t        \"\"\"List all components, ordered by usage.\"\"\"\n467\t        conn = self._get_conn()\n468\t        rows = conn.execute(\n469\t            \"\"\"\n470\t            SELECT id, name, description, html_template, version, created_at, last_used, usage_count\n471\t            FROM components\n472\t            ORDER BY usage_count DESC, last_used DESC\n473\t            LIMIT ?\n474\t            \"\"\",\n475\t            (limit,)\n476\t        ).fetchall()\n477\t\n478\t        return [\n479\t            Component(\n480\t                id=row[0],\n481\t                name=row[1],\n482\t                description=row[2],\n483\t                html_template=row[3],\n484\t                version=row[4],\n485\t                created_at=row[5],\n486\t                last_used=row[6],\n487\t                usage_count=row[7]\n488\t            )\n489\t            for row in rows\n490\t        ]\n491\t\n492\t    def get_component_history(self, component_id: str) -> List[ComponentVersion]:\n493\t        \"\"\"Get version history for a component.\"\"\"\n494\t        conn = self._get_conn()\n495\t        rows = conn.execute(\n496\t            \"\"\"\n497\t            SELECT component_id, version,      (component_id,)\n503\t        ).fetchone()\n504\t\n505\t        return [\n506\t            ComponentVersion(\n507\t                component_id=row[0],\n508\t version.\n519\t\n520\t        Args:\n521\t            component_id: The component to rollback\n522\t            to_version: The version to rollback to\n523\t\n524\t        Returns:\n525\t            The rolled-back Component, or None if not found\n526\t        \"\"\"\n527\t        conn = self._get_conn()\n528\t\n529\t        # Get the target version\n530\t        row = conn.execute(\n531\t            \"\"\"\n532\t            SELECT html_template FROM component_versions\n533\t            WHERE component_id = ? AND version = ?\n534\t            \"\"\",\n535\t            (component_id, to_version)\n536\t        ).fetchone()\n537\t\n538\t        if not row:\n539\t            return None\n540\t\n541\t        html_template = row[0]\n542\t\n543\t        # Rollback by updating with the old template as a new version\n544\t        return self.update_component(\n545\t            component_id,\n546\t            html_template,\n547\t            f\"Rollback to version {to_version}\"\n548\t        )\n549\t\n550\t    def close(self):\n551\t        \"\"\"Close the database connection.\"\"\"\n552\t        if self._conn:\n553\t            self._conn.close()\n554\t            self._conn = None\n555\t\n556\t\n557\t# Singleton instance for the application\n558\t_library_instance: Optional[ComponentLibrary] = None\n559\t\n560\t\n561\tdef get_library(db_path: str = '/home/coding/aide-de-camp/data/components.db') -> ComponentLibrary:\n562\t    \"\"\"Get or create the component library singleton.\"\"\"\n563\t    global _library_instance\n564\t    if _library_instance is None:\n565\t        _library_instance = ComponentLibrary(db_path)\n566\t    return _library_instance\n567\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_0ed8d5f4344841edaaf1822a","session_id":"d14b123e-7b4c-4e5c-94d8-a9412ba2c8a7","uuid":"b0e52a15-8dfe-4310-91b1-22d424d9eaa3","timestamp":"2026-07-23T14:35:52.218Z","subagent_type":"Explore","task_description":"Find selector and result rendering code"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_68a616deeba54ca2ac1809d4","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t\"\"\"Deterministic, no-LLM server-side card selector (the dispatch hot path).\n2\t\n3\tOn every dispatch the server picks the component to render a result\n4\t*deterministically* — the highest ``match_score`` row in\n5\t``component_usage_patterns`` for the result's ``result_type``, no LLM call —\n6\tfills that component's template with the result data per layout bucket, writes\n7\tthe rendered HTML to ``card_cache``, records the usage, and returns it for SSE\n8\tstreaming. When nothing matches (a first-ever result shape, or no score above\n9\tthreshold) the result is *flagged* (``card_fallback``) and the client renders\n10\tthe built-in generic fallback card — a novel shape never blanks the canvas.\n11\t\n12\tScope of writes (plan, Component Library → Built-in generic fallback card +\n13\tUI-Regen Agent): the hot-path renderer is the sole writer of ``card_cache`` and\n14\tthe component usage stats (``components.usage_count`` / ``last_used`` and\n15\t``component_usage_patterns``). It NEVER writes component definitions\n16\t(``components`` rows, ``component_versions``, ``component_tags``) — that is the\n17\tUI-regen agent's job alone, so the two never race on a component's identity.\n18\t\"\"\"\n19\t\n20\timport html\n21\timport re\n22\tfrom dataclasses import dataclass\n23\tfrom logging import getLogger\n24\tfrom typing import Any, Optional\n25\t\n26\tfrom ..components.library import Component, ComponentLibrary, get_library\n27\t\n28\tlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n29\t\n30\t# A component_usage_patterns row must clear this to be selected (plan: \"highest\n31\t# match_score ... no LLM\"; below this the result falls to the built-in fallback).\n32\tDEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD = 0.7\n33\t\n34\t# The hot path renders one card per intent thread at a single layout bucket.\n35\tDEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET = \"normal\"\n36\t\n37\t# Flat ``{{field.path}}`` token (no loops/conditionals) — the same substitution\n38\t# contract the UI-regen generator is told to emit (see ui_regen.py).\n39\t_PLACEHOLDER = re.compile(r\"\\{\\{([^}]+)\\}\\}\")\n40\t\n41\t\n42\tdef derive_result_type(\n43\t    intent_type: Optional[str],\n44\t    project_slug: Optional[str],\n45\t    lookup_kind: Optional[str] = None,\n46\t) -> str:\n47\t    \"\"\"Derive the deterministic card-selector key written to ``results.result_type``.\n48\t\n49\t    - intent-derived: ``\"{intent_type}:{project_slug}\"`` — one per intent thread\n50\t      (the aggregated thread card), never per fetch source.\n51\t    - lookup threads insert the router's ``lookup_kind``:\n52\t      ``\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{project_slug}\"`` (e.g. ``lookup:logs:ibkr-mcp`` vs\n53\t      ``lookup:config:ibkr-mcp`` — distinct keys, distinct cards).\n54\t    - monitoring-originated rows: ``\"monitoring:{project_slug}\"``.\n55\t\n56\t    A missing project slug collapses to ``\"general\"`` so the key is always a\n57\t    non-empty, colon-segmented string the selector can index on.\n58\t    \"\"\"\n59\t    slug = project_slug or \"general\"\n60\t    itype = intent_type or \"status\"\n61\t    if itype == \"monitoring\":\n62\t        return f\"monitoring:{slug}\"\n63\t    if itype == \"lookup\" and lookup_kind:\n64\t        return f\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{slug}\"\n65\t    return f\"{itype}:{slug}\"\n66\t\n67\t\n68\t@dataclass\n69\tclass RenderOutcome:\n70\t    \"\"\"Result of a hot-path render: either a real component render, or a fallback flag.\n71\t\n72\t    ``rendered_html`` is None exactly when no component matched (the client must\n73\t    render the built-in generic fallback card). ``card_fallback`` is the boolean\n74\t    mirror persisted onto the result row so loadTopics()/the SSE consumer both\n75\t    see the same decision without re-running the selector.\n76\t    \"\"\"\n77\t\n78\t    rendered_html: Optional[str]\n79\t    component_id: Optional[str]\n80\t    card_fallback: bool\n81\t    layout_bucket: str\n82\t\n83\t\n84\tclass HotPathRenderer:\n85\t    \"\"\"Deterministic server-side card selector — never on the LLM path.\n86\t\n87\t    Selection is a single ``component_usage_patterns`` lookup on\n88\t    ``result_type``; on a match it fills the template (escaping every\n89\t    interpolated value — the render-path escaping boundary), writes\n90\t    ``card_cache``, and bumps the usage stats. On no match it returns a\n91\t    fallback outcome and writes nothing to the component DB.\n92\t    \"\"\"\n93\t\n94\t    def __init__(\n95\t        self,\n96\t        library: Optional[ComponentLibrary] = None,\n97\t        match_threshold: float = DEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD,\n98\t        layout_bucket: str = DEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET,\n99\t    ):\n100\t        self.library = library or get_library()\n101\t        self.match_threshold = match_threshold\n102\t        self.layout_bucket = layout_bucket\n103\t\n104\t    def render(\n105\t        self,\n106\t        result_id: str,\n107\t        result_type: str,\n108\t        result_data: dict[str, Any],\n109\t        summary: Optional[str] = None,\n110\t        layout_bucket: Optional[str] = None,\n111\t    ) -> RenderOutcome:\n112\t        \"\"\"Select + render a card for one result. Deterministic, no LLM.\n113\t\n114\t        On a match: fills the template (escaped), writes card_cache keyed\n115\t        ``(result_id, component_id, layout_bucket)``, and records the usage\n116\t        pattern + usage stats. On no match / below threshold: returns a fallback\n117\t        outcome and writes nothing to the component DB (the client renders the\n118\t        built-in generic fallback card from result.data + summary).\n119\t        \"\"\"\n120\t        bucket = layout_bucket or self.layout_bucket\n121\t\n122\t        component = self.library.select_component_for_result_type(\n123\t            result_type, self.match_threshold\n124\t        )\n125\t        if component is None:\n126\t            logger.debug(\n127\t                \"hot-path fallback: no component matched result_type=%s \"\n128\t                \"(threshold %.2f) for result %s\",\n129\t                result_type,\n130\t                self.match_threshold,\n131\t                result_id,\n132\t            )\n133\t            return RenderOutcome(\n134\t                rendered_html=None,\n135\t                component_id=None,\n136\t                card_fallback=True,\n137\t                layout_bucket=bucket,\n138\t            )\n139\t\n140\t        rendered_html = fill_template(component.html_template, result_data)\n141\t\n142\t        # The server is the sole writer of card_cache + usage stats (write-scope\n143\t        # separation). cache_card bumps components.usage_count/last_used;\n144\t        # record_usage_pattern updates component_usage_patterns (sample_count,\n145\t        # last_matched, running match_score). Neither touches component\n146\t        # definitions — that is the UI-regen agent's job.\n147\t        self.library.cache_card(\n148\t            result_id, component.id, component.version, bucket, rendered_html\n149\t        )\n150\t        # A hot-path match is a confirmed, high-confidence usage — record at 1.0\n151\t        # so reliably-matching components trend toward the top of the selector.\n152\t        self.library.record_usage_pattern(component.id, result_type, match_score=1.0)\n153\t\n154\t        logger.info(\n155\t            \"hot-path rendered result %s with component %s v%s (result_type=%s)\",\n156\t            result_id,\n157\t            component.id,\n158\t            component.version,\n159\t            result_type,\n160\t        )\n161\t        return RenderOutcome(\n162\t            rendered_html=rendered_html,\n163\t            component_id=component.id,\n164\t            card_fallback=False,\n165\t            layout_bucket=bucket,\n166\t        )\n167\t\n168\t\n169\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n170\t# Template fill — the render-path escaping boundary\n171\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n172\t\n173\tdef fill_template(template: str, result_data: dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n174\t    \"\"\"Flat dot-path substitution with HTML escaping at fill time.\n175\t\n176\t    Every ``{{field.path}}`` token is replaced with the HTML-escaped value at\n177\t    that dot-path (list indices via numeric segments, e.g. ``{{pods.0.name}}``).\n178\t    Unknown paths resolve to \"\". This is the escaping boundary the plan binds\n179\t    every server-filled card to (UI-Regen Agent → \"Escaping contract\"): a\n180\t    markup-looking log line interpolated here renders as literal text instead of\n181\t    breaking layout or executing in the canvas. No loops or conditionals — the\n182\t    generator is instructed to emit only flat placeholders.\n183\t    \"\"\"\n184\t    if not template:\n185\t        return \"\"\n186\t\n187\t    def replace_value(match: re.Match) -> str:\n188\t        path = match.group(1).strip()\n189\t        value = _get_nested_value(result_data, path)\n190\t        if value is None:\n191\t            return \"\"\n192\t        return html.escape(str(value), quote=False)\n193\t\n194\t    return _PLACEHOLDER.sub(replace_value, template)\n195\t\n196\t\n197\tdef _get_nested_value(data: dict[str, Any], path: str) -> Any:\n198\t    \"\"\"Resolve a dot-path against a dict/list (numeric segments index lists).\"\"\"\n199\t    value: Any = data\n200\t    for key in path.split(\".\"):\n201\t        if isinstance(value, dict):\n202\t            value = value.get(key)\n203\t        elif isinstance(value, list) and key.isdigit() and int(key) < len(value):\n204\t            value = value[int(key)]\n205\t        else:\n206\t            return None\n207\t    return value\n208\t\n209\t\n210\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n211\t# Singleton\n212\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n213\t\n214\t_renderer: Optional[HotPathRenderer] = None\n215\t\n216\t\n217\tdef get_renderer(\n218\t    library: Optional[ComponentLibrary] = None,\n219\t    reset: bool = False,\n220\t) -> HotPathRenderer:\n221\t    \"\"\"Get or create the process-wide hot-path renderer singleton.\n222\t\n223\t    Pass ``library=`` (and ``reset=True``) to rebind it — how tests point the\n224\t    renderer at an isolated component DB without touching the production\n225\t    ``data/components.db`` singleton.\n226\t    \"\"\"\n227\t    global _renderer\n228\t    if _renderer is None or reset or library is not None:\n229\t        _renderer = HotPathRenderer(library=library)\n230\t    return _renderer\n231\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_0ed8d5f4344841edaaf1822a","session_id":"d14b123e-7b4c-4e5c-94d8-a9412ba2c8a7","uuid":"457c54cc-b707-4915-b909-78e79dbbfa3a","timestamp":"2026-07-23T14:35:52.363Z","subagent_type":"Explore","task_description":"Find It NEVER writes component definitions\n16\t(``components`` rows, ``component_versions``, ``component_tags``) — that is the\n17\tUI-regen agent's job alone, so the two never race on a component's identity.\n18\t\"\"\"\n19\t\n20\timport html\n21\timport re\n22\tfrom dataclasses import dataclass\n23\tfrom logging import getLogger\n24\tfrom typing import Any, Optional\n25\t\n26\tfrom ..components.library import Component, ComponentLibrary, get_library\n27\t\n28\tlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n29\t\n30\t# A component_usage_patterns row must clear this to be selected (plan: \"highest\n31\t# match_score ... no LLM\"; below this the result falls to the built-in fallback).\n32\tDEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD = 0.7\n33\t\n34\t# The hot path renders one card per intent thread at a single layout bucket.\n35\tDEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET = \"normal\"\n36\t\n37\t# Flat ``{{field.path}}`` token (no loops/conditionals) — the same substitution\n38\t# contract the UI-regen generator is told to emit (see ui_regen.py).\n39\t_PLACEHOLDER = re.compile(r\"\\{\\{([^}]+)\\}\\}\")\n40\t\n41\t\n42\tdef derive_result_type(\n43\t    intent_type: Optional[str],\n44\t    project_slug: Optional[str],\n45\t    lookup_kind: Optional[str] = None,\n46\t) -> str:\n47\t    \"\"\"Derive the deterministic card-selector key written to ``results.result_type``.\n48\t\n49\t    - intent-derived: ``\"{intent_type}:{project_slug}\"`` — one per intent thread\n50\t      (the aggregated thread card), never per fetch source.\n51\t    - lookup threads insert the router's ``lookup_kind``:\n52\t      ``\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{project_slug}\"`` (e.g. ``lookup:logs:ibkr-mcp`` vs\n53\t      ``lookup:config:ibkr-mcp`` — distinct keys, distinct cards).\n54\t    - monitoring-originated rows: ``\"monitoring:{project_slug}\"``.\n55\t\n56\t    A missing project slug collapses to ``\"general\"`` so the key is always a\n57\t    non-empty, colon-segmented string the selector can index on.\n58\t    \"\"\"\n59\t    slug = project_slug or \"general\"\n60\t    itype = intent_type or \"status\"\n61\t    if itype == \"monitoring\":\n62\t        return f\"monitoring:{slug}\"\n63\t    if itype == \"lookup\" and lookup_kind:\n64\t        return f\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{slug}\"\n65\t    return f\"{itype}:{slug}\"\n66\t\n67\t\n68\t@dataclass\n69\tclass RenderOutcome:\n70\t    \"\"\"Result of a hot-path render: either a real component render, or a fallback flag.\n71\t\n72\t    ``rendered_html`` is None exactly when no component matched (the client must\n73\t    render the built-in generic fallback card). ``card_fallback`` is the boolean\n74\t    mirror persisted onto the result row so loadTopics()/the SSE consumer both\n75\t    see the same decision without re-running the selector.\n76\t    \"\"\"\n77\t\n78\t    rendered_html: Optional[str]\n79\t    component_id: Optional[str]\n80\t    card_fallback: bool\n81\t    layout_bucket: str\n82\t\n83\t\n84\tclass HotPathRenderer:\n85\t    \"\"\"Deterministic server-side card selector — never on the LLM path.\n86\t\n87\t    Selection is a single ``component_usage_patterns`` lookup on\n88\t    ``result_type``; on a match it fills the template (escaping every\n89\t    interpolated value — the render-path escaping boundary), writes\n90\t    ``card_cache``, and bumps the usage stats. On no match it returns a\n91\t    fallback outcome and writes nothing to the component DB.\n92\t    \"\"\"\n93\t\n94\t    def __init__(\n95\t        self,\n96\t        library: Optional[ComponentLibrary] = None,\n97\t        match_threshold: float = DEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD,\n98\t        layout_bucket: str = DEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET,\n99\t    ):\n100\t        self.library = library or get_library()\n101\t        self.match_threshold = match_threshold\n102\t        self.layout_bucket = layout_bucket\n103\t\n104\t    def render(\n105\t        self,\n106\t        result_id: str,\n107\t        result_type: str,\n108\t        result_data: dict[str, Any],\n109\t        summary: Optional[str] = None,\n110\t        layout_bucket: Optional[str] = None,\n111\t    ) -> RenderOutcome:\n112\t        \"\"\"Select + render a card for one result. Deterministic, no LLM.\n113\t\n114\t        On a match: fills the template (escaped), writes card_cache keyed\n115\t        ``(result_id, component_id, layout_bucket)``, and records the usage\n116\t        pattern + usage stats. On no match / below threshold: returns a fallback\n117\t        outcome and writes nothing to the component DB (the client renders the\n118\t        built-in generic fallback card from result.data + summary).\n119\t        \"\"\"\n120\t        bucket = layout_bucket or self.layout_bucket\n121\t\n122\t        component = self.library.select_component_for_result_type(\n123\t            result_type, self.match_threshold\n124\t        )\n125\t        if component is None:\n126\t            logger.debug(\n127\t                \"hot-path fallback: no component matched result_type=%s \"\n128\t                \"(threshold %.2f) for result %s\",\n129\t                result_type,\n130\t                self.match_threshold,\n131\t                result_id,\n132\t            )\n133\t            return RenderOutcome(\n134\t                rendered_html=None,\n135\t                component_id=None,\n136\t                card_fallback=True,\n137\t                layout_bucket=bucket,\n138\t            )\n139\t\n140\t        rendered_html = fill_template(component.html_template, result_data)\n141\t\n142\t        # The server is the sole writer of card_cache + usage stats (write-scope\n143\t        # separation). cache_card bumps components.usage_count/last_used;\n144\t        # record_usage_pattern updates component_usage_patterns (sample_count,\n145\t        # last_matched, running match_score). Neither touches component\n146\t        # definitions — that is the UI-regen agent's job.\n147\t        self.library.cache_card(\n148\t            result_id, component.id, component.version, bucket, rendered_html\n149\t        )\n150\t        # A hot-path match is a confirmed, high-confidence usage — record at 1.0\n151\t        # so reliably-matching components trend toward the top of the selector.\n152\t        self.library.record_usage_pattern(component.id, result_type, match_score=1.0)\n153\t\n154\t        logger.info(\n155\t            \"hot-path rendered result %s with component %s v%s (result_type=%s)\",\n156\t            result_id,\n157\t            component.id,\n158\t            component.version,\n159\t            result_type,\n160\t        )\n161\t        return RenderOutcome(\n162\t            rendered_html=rendered_html,\n163\t            component_id=component.id,\n164\t            card_fallback=False,\n165\t            layout_bucket=bucket,\n166\t        )\n167\t\n168\t\n169\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n170\t# Template fill — the render-path escaping boundary\n171\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n172\t\n173\tdef fill_template(template: str, result_data: dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n174\t    \"\"\"Flat dot-path substitution with HTML escaping at fill time.\n175\t\n176\t    Every ``{{field.path}}`` token is replaced with the HTML-escaped value at\n177\t    that dot-path (list indices via numeric segments, e.g. ``{{pods.0.name}}``).\n178\t    Unknown paths resolve to \"\". This is the escaping boundary the plan binds\n179\t    every server-filled card to (UI-Regen Agent → \"Escaping contract\"): a\n180\t    markup-looking log line interpolated here renders as literal text instead of\n181\t    breaking layout or executing in the canvas. No loops or conditionals — the\n182\t    generator is instructed to emit only flat placeholders.\n183\t    \"\"\"\n184\t    if not template:\n185\t        return \"\"\n186\t\n187\t    def no-LLM server-side card selector (the dispatch hot path).\n\nOn every dispatch the server picks the component to render a result\n*deterministically* — the highest ``match_score`` row in\n``component_usage_patterns`` for the result's ``result_type``, no LLM call —\nfills that component's template with the result data per layout bucket, writes\nthe rendered HTML to ``card_cache``, records the usage, and returns it for SSE\nstreaming. When nothing matches (a first-ever result shape, or no score above\nthreshold) the result is *flagged* (``card_fallback``) and the client renders\nthe built-in generic fallback card — a novel shape never blanks the canvas.\n\nScope of writes (plan, Component Library → Built-in generic fallback card +\nUI-Regen Agent): the hot-path renderer is the sole writer of ``card_cache`` and\nthe component usage stats (``components.usage_count`` / ``last_used`` and\n``component_usage_patterns``). It NEVER writes component definitions\n(``components`` rows, ``component_versions``, ``component_tags``) — that is the\nUI-regen agent's job alone, so the two never race on a component's identity.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport html\nimport re\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom typing import Any, Optional\n\nfrom ..components.library import Component, ComponentLibrary, get_library\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n# A component_usage_patterns row must clear this to be selected (plan: \"highest\n# match_score ... no LLM\"; below this the result falls to the built-in fallback).\nDEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD = 0.7\n\n# The hot path renders one card per intent thread at a single layout bucket.\nDEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET = \"normal\"\n\n# Flat ``{{field.path}}`` token (no loops/conditionals) — the same substitution\n# contract the UI-regen generator is told to emit (see ui_regen.py).\n_PLACEHOLDER = re.compile(r\"\\{\\{([^}]+)\\}\\}\")\n\n\ndef derive_result_type(\n    intent_type: Optional[str],\n    project_slug: Optional[str],\n    lookup_kind: Optional[str] = None,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Derive the deterministic card-selector key written to ``results.result_type``.\n\n    - intent-derived: ``\"{intent_type}:{project_slug}\"`` — one per intent thread\n      (the aggregated thread card), never per fetch source.\n    - lookup threads insert the router's ``lookup_kind``:\n      ``\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{project_slug}\"`` (e.g. ``lookup:logs:ibkr-mcp`` vs\n      ``lookup:config:ibkr-mcp`` — distinct keys, distinct cards).\n    - monitoring-originated rows: ``\"monitoring:{project_slug}\"``.\n\n    A missing project slug collapses to ``\"general\"`` so the key is always a\n    non-empty, colon-segmented string the selector can index on.\n    \"\"\"\n    slug = project_slug or \"general\"\n    itype = intent_type or \"status\"\n    if itype == \"monitoring\":\n        return f\"monitoring:{slug}\"\n    if itype == \"lookup\" and lookup_kind:\n        return f\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{slug}\"\n    return f\"{itype}:{slug}\"\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass RenderOutcome:\n    \"\"\"Result of a hot-path render: either a real component render, or a fallback flag.\n\n    ``rendered_html`` is None exactly when no component matched (the client must\n    render the built-in generic fallback card). ``card_fallback`` is the boolean\n    mirror persisted onto the result row so loadTopics()/the SSE consumer both\n    see the same decision without re-running the selector.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    rendered_html: Optional[str]\n    component_id: Optional[str]\n    card_fallback: bool\n    layout_bucket: str\n\n\nclass HotPathRenderer:\n    \"\"\"Deterministic server-side card selector — never on the LLM path.\n\n    Selection is a single ``component_usage_patterns`` lookup on\n    ``result_type``; on a match it fills the template (escaping every\n    interpolated value — the render-path escaping boundary), writes\n    ``card_cache``, and bumps the usage stats. On no match it returns a\n    fallback outcome and writes nothing to the component DB.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        library: Optional[ComponentLibrary] = None,\n        match_threshold: float = DEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD,\n        layout_bucket: str = DEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET,\n    ):\n        self.library = library or get_library()\n        self.match_threshold = match_threshold\n        self.layout_bucket = layout_bucket\n\n    def render(\n        self,\n        result_id: str,\n        result_type: str,\n        result_data: dict[str, Any],\n        summary: Optional[str] = None,\n        layout_bucket: Optional[str] = None,\n    ) -> RenderOutcome:\n        \"\"\"Select + render a card for one result. Deterministic, no LLM.\n\n        On a match: fills the template (escaped), writes card_cache keyed\n        ``(result_id, component_id, layout_bucket)``, and records the usage\n        pattern + usage stats. On no match / below threshold: returns a fallback\n        outcome and writes nothing to the component DB (the client renders the\n        built-in generic fallback card from result.data + summary).\n        \"\"\"\n        bucket = layout_bucket or self.layout_bucket\n\n        component = self.library.select_component_for_result_type(\n            result_type, self.match_threshold\n        )\n        if component is None:\n            logger.debug(\n                \"hot-path fallback: no component matched result_type=%s \"\n                \"(threshold %.2f) for result %s\",\n                result_type,\n                self.match_threshold,\n                result_id,\n            )\n            return RenderOutcome(\n                rendered_html=None,\n                component_id=None,\n                card_fallback=True,\n                layout_bucket=bucket,\n            )\n\n        rendered_html = fill_template(component.html_template, result_data)\n\n        # The server is the sole writer of card_cache + usage stats (write-scope\n        # separation). cache_card bumps components.usage_count/last_used;\n        # record_usage_pattern updates component_usage_patterns (sample_count,\n        # last_matched, running match_score). Neither touches component\n        # definitions — that is the UI-regen agent's job.\n        self.library.cache_card(\n            result_id, component.id, component.version, bucket, rendered_html\n        )\n        # A hot-path match is a confirmed, high-confidence usage — record at 1.0\n        # so reliably-matching components trend toward the top of the selector.\n        self.library.record_usage_pattern(component.id, result_type, match_score=1.0)\n\n        logger.info(\n            \"hot-path rendered result %s with component %s v%s (result_type=%s)\",\n            result_id,\n            component.id,\n            component.version,\n            result_type,\n        )\n        return RenderOutcome(\n            rendered_html=rendered_html,\n            component_id=component.id,\n            card_fallback=False,\n            layout_bucket=bucket,\n        )\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Template fill — the render-path escaping boundary\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef fill_template(template: str, result_data: dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Flat dot-path substitution with HTML escaping at fill time.\n\n    Every ``{{field.path}}`` token is replaced with the HTML-escaped value at\n    that dot-path (list indices via numeric segments, e.g. ``{{pods.0.name}}``).\n    Unknown paths resolve to \"\". This is the escaping boundary the plan binds\n    every server-filled card to (UI-Regen Agent → \"Escaping contract\"): a\n    markup-looking log line interpolated here renders as literal text instead of\n    breaking layout or executing in the canvas. No loops or conditionals — the\n    generator is instructed to emit only flat placeholders.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not template:\n        return \"\"\n\n    def replace_value(match: re.Match) -> str:\n        path = match.group(1).strip()\n        value = _get_nested_value(result_data, path)\n        if value is None:\n            return \"\"\n        return html.escape(str(value), quote=False)\n\n    return _PLACEHOLDER.sub(replace_value, template)\n\n\ndef _get_nested_value(data: dict[str, Any], path: str) -> Any:\n    \"\"\"Resolve a dot-path against a dict/list (numeric segments index lists).\"\"\"\n    value: Any = data\n    for key in path.split(\".\"):\n        if isinstance(value, dict):\n            value = value.get(key)\n        elif isinstance(value, list) and key.isdigit() and int(key) < len(value):\n            value = value[int(key)]\n        else:\n            return None\n    return value\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Singleton\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n_renderer: Optional[HotPathRenderer] = None\n\n\ndef get_renderer(\n    library: Optional[ComponentLibrary] = None,\n    reset: bool = False,\n) -> HotPathRenderer:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the process-wide hot-path renderer singleton.\n\n    Pass ``library=`` (and ``reset=True``) to rebind it — how tests point the\n    renderer at an isolated component DB without touching the production\n    ``data/components.db`` singleton.\n    \"\"\"\n    global _renderer\n    if _renderer is None or reset or library is not None:\n        _renderer = HotPathRenderer(library=library)\n    return _renderer\n","numLines":231,"startLine":1,"totalLines":231}}}

          </div>\n847\t        </div>\n848\t    </div>\n849\t\n850\t    <!-- Render helpers (createTopicCard + friends) — loaded before the app\n851\t         script so they're available as globals generateId() {\n873\t            return 'adc-' + Math.random().toString(36).substr(2, 16);\n874\t        }\n875\t\n876\t        // createTopicCard / escapeHtml / formatStaleness / getStalenessLevel
