showWorkloadSummary(summary) {\n890\t            const el = document.getElementById('workloadSummary');\n891\t            const parts = [];\n892\t\n893\t            if (summary.pending_intents > 0) {\n894\t                parts.push(`<strong>${summary.pending_intents}</strong> pending intents`);\n895\t            }\n896\t            if (summary.new_results > 0) {\n897\t                parts.push(`<strong>${summary.new_results}</strong> new results`);\n898\t            }\n899\t            if (summary.unresolved_exceptions > 0) {\n900\t                parts.push(`<strong>${summary.unresolved_exceptions}</strong> unresolved exceptions`);\n901\t            }\n902\t\n903\t            if (parts.length > 0) {\n904\t                el.innerHTML = '📊 While you were away: ' + parts.join(', ');\n905\t                el.classList.add('show');\n906\t            } else {\n907\t                el.classList.remove('show');\n908\t            }\n909\t        }\n910\t\n911\t        async function registerSurface() {\n912\t            try {\n913\t                const response = await fetch('/api/v1/surfaces/register', {\n914\t                    method: 'POST',\n915\t                    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n916\t                    body: JSON.stringify({\n917\t                        session_id: sessionId,\n918\t                        surface_type: surfaceType\n919\t                    })\n920\t                });\n921\t\n922\t                if (!response.ok) throw new Error('Failed to register surface');\n923\t\n924\t                const data = await response.json();\n925\t                surfaceId = data.surface_id;\n926\t                return surfaceId;\n927\t            } catch (error) {\n928\t                console.error('Error registering surface:', error);\n929\t                throw error;\n930\t            }\n931\t        }\n932\t\n933\t        // Registry project list for the first-run welcome card\n934\t        // (config/registry.yaml → GET /api/v1/registry). DB/result independent —\n935\t        // the welcome card renders even against an empty component store. Cached\n936\t        // after the first fetch so the per-SSE-event reloads don't refetch it.\n937\t        let welcomeProjects = null;\n938\t\n939\t        async function loadWelcomeProjects() {\n940\t            if (welcomeProjects) return welcomeProjects;\n941\t            try {\n942\t                const resp = await fetch('/api/v1/registry');\n943\t                if (resp.ok) {\n944\t                    const data = await resp.json();\n945\t                    welcomeProjects = (data && data.projects) || [];\n946\t                    return welcomeProjects;\n947\t                }\n948\t            } catch (error) {\n949\t                console.error('Error loading registry for welcome card:', error);\n950\t            }\n951\t            // Graceful fallback: an empty list still renders the welcome card\n952\t            // (with default example utterances), so a wedged registry endpoint\n953\t            // never leaves the canvas bare.\n954\t            welcomeProjects = [];\n955\t            return welcomeProjects;\n956\t        }\n957\t\n958\t        async function loadTopics() {\n959\t            try {\n960\t                const response = await fetch(`/api/v1/sessions/${sessionId}/topics`);\n961\t                if (!response.ok) return;\n962\t\n963\t                const data = await response.json();\n964\t                const container = document.getElementById('topicsContainer');\n965\t                const cards = data.cards || [];\n966\t\n967\t                // Fresh session (zero topics): show the first-run welcome card\n968\t                // instead of the bare empty-state. Its project list + example\n969\t                // utterances come from the registry (never the DB), so we only\n970\t                // need to fetch it on the zero-card branch. The welcome card is\n971\t                // dropped — not duplicated — the moment the first real result\n972\t                // lands: the next SSE result_created → loadTopics() reload\n973\t                // yields a non-empty card set, so buildContainerChildren()\n974\t                // returns topic cards only (clearing the welcome card).\n975\t                const projects = cards.length ? [] : await loadWelcomeProjects();\n976\t\n977\t                container.innerHTML = '';\n978\t                buildContainerChildren(cards, projects).forEach(node => {\n979\t                    container.appendChild(node);\n980\t                });\n981\t            } catch (error) {\n982\t                console.error('Error loading topics:', error);\n983\t            }\n984\t        }\n985\t\n986\t        function connectSSE() {\n987\t            updateConnectionStatus('connecting', 'Connecting...');\n988\t\n989\t            const url = EventSource(url);\n991\t\n992\t            eventSource.onopen = () => {\n993\t                updateConnectionStatus('', 'Connected');\n994\t                loadTopics();\n995\t            };\n996\t\n997\t            eventSource.onerror = (error) => {\n998\t                console.error('SSE error:', error);\n999\t                updateConnectionStatus('disconnected', 'Disconnected');\n1000\t            };\n1001\t\n1002\t            eventSource.addEventListener('connected', (event) => {\n1003\t                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n1004\t                console.log('SSE connected:', data);\n1005\t            });\n1006\t\n1007\t            eventSource.addEventListener('workload_summary', (event) => {\n1008\t                const summary = didn't\n1013\t            // include them (e.g., the response arrived before the intent was routed).\n1014\t            eventSource.addEventListener('dispatch_ack', (event) => {\n1015\t data.utterance;\n1019\t\n1020\t                if (!utteranceId || !intentIds || !Array.isArray(intentIds)) return;\n1021\t\n1022\t                // Check if there's an existing placeholder to && placeholder.parentNode) {\n1034\t                    // Replace the placeholder with the thread cards\n1035\t                    const frag = document.createDocumentFragment();\n1036\t                    threadCards.forEach(function (c) { frag.appendChild(c); });\n1037\t                    placeholder.parentNode.replaceChild(frag, placeholder);\n1038\t                } else {\n1039\t                    // No placeholder (test scenario or race condition): prepend thread cards\n1040\t                    const frag = document.createDocumentFragment();\n1041\t                    threadCards.forEach(function (c) { frag.appendChild(c); });\n1042\t                    container.insertBefore(frag, container.firstChild);\n1043\t                }\n1044\t\n1045\t                // No        if (!threadId) {\n1055\t                    console.warn('thread_progress: missing thread_id in event data');\n1056\t                    return;\n1057\t                }\n1058\t\n1059\t                // Find the pending thread card by thread_id\n1060\t                const threadCard = document.querySelector('[data-pending-id=\"' + threadId + '\"]');\n1061\t                if (!threadCard) {\n1062\t                    console.warn('thread_progress: threadCard not found for thread_id=' + threadId);\n1063\t                    return;\n1064\t                }\n1065\t                if         return;\n1068\t                }\n1069\t                if (typeof _setProgress !== 'function') {\n1070\t                    console.warn('thread_progress: _setProgress is not a function');\n1071\t                    return;\n1072\t                }\n1073\t\n1074\t                // Pass progress message (e.g., '3/5 sources in') to _setProgress()\n1075\t                const completed = data.completed;\n1076\t                const total = data.total;\n1077\t                _setProgress(threadCard, { completed: completed, total: total });\n1078\t            });\n1079\t\n1080\t            // Per-source progress update for a thread\n1081\t            eventSource.addEventListener('progress_update', (event) => {\n1082\t                const data = data.total;\n1086\t\n1087\t                if (!intentId) return;\n1088\t\n1089\t                // Find the pending thread card by intent_id\n1090\t                const threadCard =      _setProgress(threadCard, { completed: completed, total: total });\n1104\t            });\n1105\t\n1106\t            eventSource.addEventListener('result_created', (event) => {\n1107\t {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_c5373cf02a9d45ea8d9c7028","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t/*\n2\t * ADC Canvas — rendering helpers.\n3\t *\n4\t * Pure functions that turn a /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics card dict into a DOM\n5\t * topic-card element, and that build the four BUILT-IN card families the plan\n6\t * ships as fixed templates in this served frontend (Component Library →\n7\t * \"Built-in cards\"): (1) generic fallback, (2) first-run welcome card, (3)\n8\t * pending/ack cards (submit-time placeholder, per-thread pending with\n9\t * per-source progress, aged-pending treatment), and (4) the error/clarification\n10\t * family from Degraded-State UX. They are kept in this separate, loadable\n11\t * module (rather than inline in index.html) so the render contract can be\n12\t * verified headlessly — tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js and\n13\t * tests/e2e/canvas_builtin_runner.js load this exact file under a minimal DOM\n14\t * shim and assert the rendered HTML, with no browser or network required.\n15\t *\n16\t * ESCAPING CONTRACT (the render-path escaping contract, see plan UI-Regen Agent\n17\t * \"Escaping contract\"): every dynamic value — registry descriptions, utterance\n18\t * text, per-source names, error detail, elapsed strings, LLM/worker-authored\n19\t * free text — is interpolated through escapeHtml(), which sets it via a text\n20\t * node (textContent → the browser's own escaper). No dynamic value is ever\n21\t * spliced raw into innerHTML. The built-in builders below all go through the\n22\t * same escapeHtml() the topic card uses, so they are bound by the identical\n23\t * escaping contract.\n24\t *\n25\t * In the browser, index.html loads this via <script src=\"/canvas.js\"> before\n26\t * the inline app script, so these declarations are globals just like they were\n27\t * when they lived inline. In Node, the trailing export shim exposes them via\n28\t * module.exports for the DOM tests.\n29\t */\n30\t\n31\t// Node.js compatibility: ensure document is available in Node for headless tests\n32\t// When loaded in Node (module.exports exists), make the global document accessible\n33\t// to the el() helper function which uses document.createElement() and .createTextNode()\n34\tif (typeof module !== 'undefined' && module.exports && typeof document === 'undefined') {\n35\t    var document = global.document;\n36\t}\n37\t\n38\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n39\t// Tunables (exported so tests can assert against the same constants)\n40\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n41\t\n42\t// Hot-path intents flag at 30s pending (their budget is 3s) — see plan, The\n43\t// Async Path → Visible aging + Degraded-State UX aged-pending row. This flag is\n44\t// applied PURELY client-side from the local placeholder's creation time so it\n45\t// survives a hung/wedged server (no SSE dependency).\n46\tvar PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS = 30000;\n47\t\n48\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n49\t// Existing topic-card render helpers\n50\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n51\t\n52\tfunction formatStaleness(seconds) {\n53\t    if (seconds < 60) return 'just now';\n54\t    if (seconds < 3600) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 60)}m ago`;\n55\t    if (seconds < 86400) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 3600)}h ago`;\n56\t    return `${Math.floor(seconds / 86400)}d ago`;\n57\t}\n58\t\n59\tfunction getStalenessLevel(seconds) {\n60\t    if (seconds < 600) return 'fresh';      // 10 minutes\n61\t    if (seconds < 3600) return 'stale';     // 1 hour\n62\t    return 'very-stale';\n63\t}\n64\t\n65\t/**\n66\t * Build a topic card from topic data.\n67\t *\n68\t * @param {TopicCardData} cardData - Topic card data structure\n69\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .topic-card element (or fallback-card element if card_fallback is set)\n70\t */\n71\tfunction createTopicCard(cardData) {\n72\t    const topic = cardData.topic;\n73\t    const staleness = cardData.staleness;\n74\t    const latestResult = cardData.latest_result;\n75\t\n76\t    // When the result has card_fallback=True, render the built-in generic fallback card\n77\t    // instead of the standard topic card. The fallback card is a complete card showing\n78\t    // result.data as a key/value grid (plan: Component Library → \"Built-in generic\n79\t    // fallback card\"). Topic metadata is preserved via data attributes for staleness\n80\t    // tracking and querying, but visually the card is the fallback presentation.\n81\t    if (latestResult && latestResult.card_fallback) {\n82\t        const fallbackCard = createFallbackCard(latestResult);\n83\t        // Preserve topic metadata for staleness tracking and querying\n84\t        fallbackCard.dataset.topicId = topic.id;\n85\t        fallbackCard.dataset.topicType = topic.type || 'adhoc';\n86\t        fallbackCard.dataset.cardFallback = '1';\n87\t        return fallbackCard;\n88\t    }\n89\t\n90\t    const card = document.createElement('div');\n91\t    card.className = 'topic-card';\n92\t    card.dataset.topicId = topic.id;\n93\t\n94\t    const stalenessLevel = getStalenessLevel(staleness.seconds);\n95\t    card.classList.add(stalenessLevel);\n96\t\n97\t    const typeClass = topic.type || 'adhoc';\n98\t\n99\t    // Expose the topic type as a data attribute alongside data-topic-id so cards\n100\t    // are queryable by type with a robust, stable selector (e.g.\n101\t    // [data-topic-type=\"research\"]) rather than relying on class names. The DOM\n102\t    // verification suites assert both dataset attributes are present per card.\n103\t    card.dataset.topicType = typeClass;\n104\t\n105\t    let html = `\n106\t        <div class=\"topic-header\">\n107\t            <div class=\"topic-label\">${escapeHtml(topic.label)}</div>\n108\t            <div>\n109\t                <span class=\"topic-type ${typeClass}\">${escapeHtml(topic.type || 'adhoc')}</span>\n110\t                ${stalenessLevel !== 'fresh' ? `<span class=\"stale-badge ${stalenessLevel}\">STALE</span>` : ''}\n111\t            </div>\n112\t        </div>\n113\t    `;\n114\t\n115\t    // Add latest result if available\n116\t    if (latestResult) {\n117\t        const urgencyClass = latestResult.urgency || 'normal';\n118\t        html += `\n119\t            <div class=\"result-content\">\n120\t                <div class=\"result-summary\">\n121\t                    ${escapeHtml(latestResult.summary)}\n122\t                    <span class=\"urgency-badge ${urgencyClass}\">${escapeHtml(urgencyClass)}</span>\n123\t                </div>\n124\t        `;\n125\t\n126\t        if (latestResult.data) {\n127\t            html += `<div class=\"result-data\">${escapeHtml(JSON.stringify(latestResult.data, null, 2))}</div>`;\n128\t        }\n129\t\n130\t        html += `</div>`;\n131\t    }\n132\t\n133\t    // Add staleness indicator\n134\t    const timeAgo = formatStaleness(staleness.seconds);\n135\t    html += `\n136\t        <div class=\"staleness-indicator ${stalenessLevel}\">\n137\t            <span class=\"staleness-dot ${stalenessLevel}\"></span>\n138\t            <span>Updated ${timeAgo}</span>\n139\t        </div>\n140\t    `;\n141\t\n142\t    card.innerHTML = html;\n143\t    return card;\n144\t}\n145\t\n146\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n147\t// Shared escaping + small DOM helpers (text-node based — escaping contract)\n148\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n149\t\n150\tfunction escapeHtml(text) {\n151\t    const div = document.createElement('div');\n152\t    div.textContent = text == null ? '' : String(text);\n153\t    return div.innerHTML;\n154\t}\n155\t\n156\t/**\n157\t * Build a DOM element with tag + className + a list of children/strings.\n158\t *\n159\t * Strings are appended as TEXT NODES (never spliced into innerHTML), which is\n160\t * the escaping contract's literal form — a markup-looking log line renders as\n161\t * literal text instead of breaking layout. Use this for any value that must be\n162\t * inserted verbatim as text.\n163\t */\n164\tfunction el(tag, className, children) {\n165\t    const node = document.createElement(tag);\n166\t    if (className) node.className = className;\n167\t    (children || []).forEach(function (c) {\n168\t        if (c == null) return;\n169\t        if (typeof c === 'string' || typeof c === 'number') {\n170\t            node.appendChild(document.createTextNode(String(c)));\n171\t        } else {\n172\t            node.appendChild(c);\n173\t        }\n174\t    });\n175\t    return node;\n176\t}\n177\t\n178\t// Seconds → \"0s\" / \"12s\" / \"1m 05s\" elapsed label for pending cards.\n179\tfunction formatElapsed(ms) {\n180\t    const s = Math.max(0, Math.floor((ms || 0) / 1000));\n181\t    if (s < 60) return s + 's';\n182\t    const m = Math.floor(s / 60);\n183\t    const rem = s % 60;\n184\t    return m + 'm ' + (rem < 10 ? '0' : '') + rem + 's';\n185\t}\n186\t\n187\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n188\t// (2) First-run welcome card — built-in, zero DB dependence\n189\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n190\t\n191\t// Default example utterances used when the registry yields no project with a\n192\t// supported intent to derive one from. These never touch the component DB.\n193\tvar DEFAULT_EXAMPLE_UTTERANCES = [\n194\t    'Has the options pipeline caught up?',\n195\t    'What is the state of the ibkr mcp?',\n196\t    'Queue up a research task on recent pipeline errors — no rush.'\n197\t];\n198\t\n199\t// Map a supported intent type to an example utterance fragment, so the welcome\n200\t// card's examples are drawn from the projects the router actually knows about\n201\t// (plan: Cold start & demo seed — \"2-3 example utterances drawn from those\n202\t// projects' supported intents\").\n203\tvar INTENT_EXAMPLES = {\n204\t    status: 'What is the status of {proj}?',\n205\t    action: 'Restart the {proj} deployment.',\n206\t    brainstorm: 'Brainstorm trade-offs for {proj}.',\n207\t    lookup: 'Pull up recent logs for {proj}.',\n208\t    'task-profile': 'Queue up a research task on {proj} — no rush.',\n209\t    'self-modification': 'Add an alias to the {proj} registry entry.',\n210\t    'monitoring-config': 'Watch {proj} for pod restarts.',\n211\t    reminder: 'Remind me to check {proj} in an hour.'\n212\t};\n213\t\n214\tfunction _deriveExamples(projects) {\n215\t    const out = [];\n216\t    const seen = {};\n217\t    // Prefer projects that carry a description (registry.yaml entries) so the\n218\t    // welcome examples reference real, described projects — not bare discovered\n219\t    // repos with auto-README blurbs.\n220\t    const ordered = (projects || []).slice().sort(function (a, b) {\n221\t        const ad = a && a.description ? 0 : 1;\n222\t        const bd = b && b.description ? 0 : 1;\n223\t        return ad - bd;\n224\t    });\n225\t    ordered.forEach(function (p) {\n226\t        if (out.length >= 3) return;\n227\t        (p.intent_support || []).forEach(function (intent) {\n228\t            if (out.length >= 3 || seen[intent]) return;\n229\t            const tmpl = INTENT_EXAMPLES[intent];\n230\t            if (!tmpl) return;\n231\t            seen[intent] = true;\n232\t            out.push(tmpl.replace('{proj}', p.slug || p.name || 'the project'));\n233\t        });\n234\t    });\n235\t    return out.length ? out : DEFAULT_EXAMPLE_UTTERANCES.slice(0, 3);\n236\t}\n237\t\n238\t// Cap the welcome-card project list so a 38-repo registry doesn't overwhelm the\n239\t// first frame. The first N are shown; an \"+N more\" note counts the rest.\n240\tvar WELCOME_PROJECT_CAP = 12;\n241\t\n242\t/**\n243\t * Build the first-run welcome card.\n244\t *\n245\t * @param {Array} projects  registry entries [{slug,name,description,intent_support,aliases}]\n246\t * @param {string} [description]  one-line description of aide-de-camp\n247\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .welcome-card element (data-builtin=\"welcome\")\n248\t *\n249\t * Renders even against an empty component library / DB — the project list comes\n250\t * from config/registry.yaml (served at /api/v1/registry), not the DB. The first\n251\t * real result replaces it (the canvas drops it once any topic card exists).\n252\t */\n253\tfunction createWelcomeCard(projects, description) {\n254\t    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card welcome-card');\n255\t    card.dataset.builtin = 'welcome';\n256\t\n257\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n258\t        el('span', 'builtin-icon', ['👋']),\n259\t        el('span', 'builtin-title', ['Welcome to ADC'])\n260\t    ]));\n261\t\n262\t    card.appendChild(el('p', 'builtin-desc', [\n263\t        description || 'A single input surface that routes what you say to the right project, fetches in parallel, and renders results as live cards.'\n264\t    ]));\n265\t\n266\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-section-title', ['Registered projects']));\n267\t    const list = el('ul', 'builtin-project-list');\n268\t    (projects || []).forEach(function (p) {\n269\t        const slug = p.slug || p.name || 'project';\n270\t        const desc = p.description || '';\n271\t        const intents = (p.intent_support || []).join(' · ');\n272\t        const li = el('li', 'builtin-project');\n273\t        li.appendChild(el('strong', 'builtin-project-slug', [slug]));\n274\t        if (desc) li.appendChild(document.createTextNode(' — ' + desc));\n275\t        if (intents) li.appendChild(el('span', 'builtin-intents', [' ' + intents]));\n276\t        list.appendChild(li);\n277\t    });\n278\t    if (!(projects && projects.length)) {\n279\t        list.appendChild(el('li', 'builtin-project', ['No projects registered yet.']));\n280\t    }\n281\t    card.appendChild(list);\n282\t\n283\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-section-title', ['Try asking']));\n284\t    const examples = el('ul', 'builtin-examples');\n285\t    _deriveExamples(projects).forEach(function (ex) {\n286\t        examples.appendChild(el('li', 'builtin-example', [ex]));\n287\t    });\n288\t    card.appendChild(examples);\n289\t\n290\t    return card;\n291\t}\n292\t\n293\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n294\t// Container decision — the headlessly-testable core of loadTopics()\n295\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n296\t\n297\t/**\n298\t * Decide what the canvas topic-container shows for a given card set.\n299\t *\n300\t * This is the shared render path index.html's ``loadTopics()`` calls after\n301\t * ``GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics``: on a fresh session with zero cards it\n302\t * shows the first-run welcome card (built from the registry project list — no\n303\t * DB/result dependence); the moment any real card exists it shows only topic\n304\t * cards, which drops any welcome card (never alongside real cards). The caller\n305\t * clears the container and appends the returned nodes.\n306\t *\n307\t * Kept here (not inline in index.html) so the zero→welcome / first-result→drop\n308\t * contract is verifiable headlessly: ``tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js`` drives\n309\t * this exact function (``--container`` mode) and asserts the welcome card\n310\t * renders from an empty card set and is absent once a real card is present.\n311\t *\n312\t * @param {Array} [cards]      card dicts from /topics (empty → welcome card)\n313\t * @param {Array} [projects]   registry entries for the welcome card\n314\t * @param {string} [description] one-line description for the welcome card\n315\t * @returns {HTMLElement[]}    child nodes for the container\n316\t */\n317\tfunction buildContainerChildren(cards, projects, description) {\n318\t    if (!cards || cards.length === 0) {\n319\t        return [createWelcomeCard(projects, description)];\n320\t    }\n321\t    return cards.map(function (c) { return createTopicCard(c); });\n322\t}\n323\t\n324\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n325\t// (3) Pending/ack cards — submit-time placeholder + per-thread pending\n326\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n327\t\n328\t/**\n329\t * Build a submit-time pending placeholder.\n330\t *\n331\t * Created LOCALLY at dispatch() submit time, BEFORE any server response, so a\n332\t * hung/wedged server still leaves a card on canvas to age (plan, Escalate\n333\t * Strand → Pending/ack card render path). It splits into per-thread pending\n334\t * cards when the dispatch ack (the /dispatch HTTP response) carries intent_ids.\n335\t *\n336\t * @param {string} utterance   the raw text being dispatched\n337\t * @param {number} createdAt   Date.now() at placeholder creation (ms epoch)\n338\t * @param {string} [pendingId] stable id for this placeholder (utterance id)\n339\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .pending-card.placeholder (data-builtin=\"pending\")\n340\t */\n341\tfunction createPendingPlaceholderCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId) {\n342\t    const card = _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId, 'Sending…');\n343\t    card.classList.add('placeholder');\n344\t    card.dataset.pendingKind = 'placeholder';\n345\t    return card;\n346\t}\n347\t\n348\t/**\n349\t * Build a per-thread pending card (one per dispatch-ack intent_id). Carries the\n350\t * per-source progress state ('3/5 sources in') and the elapsed-time footer.\n351\t *\n352\t * @param {string} intentId    the intent this thread tracks (result_created key)\n353\t * @param {string} utterance   the originating utterance fragment/text\n354\t * @param {number} createdAt   ms epoch inherited from the placeholder\n355\t * @param {object} [progress]  {completed, total} initial per-source progress\n356\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .pending-card.thread (data-builtin=\"pending\")\n357\t */\n358\tfunction createPendingThreadCard(intentId, utterance, createdAt, progress) {\n359\t    const card = _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, intentId, 'Working on it…');\n360\t    card.classList.add('thread');\n361\t    card.dataset.pendingKind = 'thread';\n362\t    if (progress) _setProgress(card, progress);\n363\t    return card;\n364\t}\n365\t\n366\t/**\n367\t * Split a submit-time placeholder into per-thread pending cards on the dispatch\n368\t * ack. The /dispatch ack response carries ``intent_ids`` — one per routed\n369\t * thread — so this produces one per-thread pending card per intent_id, each\n370\t * inheriting the placeholder's ``createdAt`` (so the 30s aged timer is\n371\t * continuous across the split — the timer started at submit, not at ack) and\n372\t * the originating utterance. (plan, Escalate Strand → Pending/ack card render\n373\t * path: \"splits into per-thread pending cards when the dispatch ack arrives\".)\n374\t *\n375\t * This is the headlessly-testable core of the split: index.html's dispatch()\n376\t * calls it with the ack's intent_ids and replaces the placeholder DOM node with\n377\t * the returned cards; the Node DOM shim exercises it directly (no browser) to\n378\t * prove the split is one-card-per-thread.\n379\t *\n380\t * @param {string} utterance   the originating utterance text\n381\t * @param {number} createdAt   ms epoch inherited from the placeholder\n382\t * @param {string[]} intentIds the ack's intent_ids (one per routed thread)\n383\t * @returns {HTMLElement[]} one .pending-card.thread per intent_id\n384\t */\n385\tfunction splitPlaceholderToThreads(utterance, createdAt, intentIds) {\n386\t    return (intentIds || []).map(function (id) {\n387\t        return createPendingThreadCard(id, utterance, createdAt);\n388\t    });\n389\t}\n390\t\n391\t// Shared skeleton for both placeholder + per-thread pending cards.\n392\tfunction _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId, titleText) {\n393\t    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card pending-card');\n394\t    card.dataset.builtin = 'pending';\n395\t    card.dataset.createdAt = String(createdAt || 0);\n396\t    if (pendingId) card.dataset.pendingId = String(pendingId);\n397\t\n398\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n399\t        el('span', 'pending-spinner'),\n400\t        el('span', 'builtin-title pending-title', [titleText || 'Pending…'])\n401\t    ]));\n402\t\n403\t    if (utterance) {\n404\t        card.appendChild(el('p', 'pending-utterance', [utterance]));\n405\t    }\n406\t\n407\t    const progress = el('div', 'pending-progress');\n408\t    progress.style.display = 'none';\n409\t    card.appendChild(progress);\n410\t\n411\t    const elapsed = el('div', 'pending-elapsed', [\n412\t        formatElapsed(0) + ' elapsed'\n413\t    ]);\n414\t    card.appendChild(elapsed);\n415\t\n416\t    // Aged note (hidden until the 30s threshold) — see applyAgedTreatment().\n417\t    const agedNote = el('div', 'pending-aged-note');\n418\t    agedNote.style.display = 'none';\n419\t    agedNote.appendChild(document.createTextNode('Taking longer than expected — the server may be hung. '));\n420\t    const retry = el('button', 'pending-retry', ['Retry']);\n421\t    agedNote.appendChild(retry);\n422\t    card.appendChild(agedNote);\n423\t\n424\t    return card;\n425\t}\n426\t\n427\tfunction _setProgress(card, progress) {\n428\t    const node = card.querySelector('.pending-progress');\n429\t    if (!node || !progress) return;\n430\t    const completed = Math.max(0, parseInt(progress.completed, 10) || 0);\n431\t    const total = Math.max(0, parseInt(progress.total, 10) || 0);\n432\t    node.textContent = completed + '/' + total + ' sources in';\n433\t    node.dataset.completed = String(completed);\n434\t    node.dataset.total = String(total);\n435\t    node.style.display = total > 0 ? '' : 'none';\n436\t}\n437\t\n438\t/**\n439\t * Recompute the elapsed-time footer for a pending card against `now` (ms).\n440\t * Returns the elapsed ms. Pure: no timers, no globals beyond the card's own\n441\t * data-createdAt — so the headless harness can drive it via a mock clock.\n442\t */\n443\tfunction tickPendingElapsed(card, now) {\n444\t    const createdAt = parseInt(card.dataset.createdAt, 10) || 0;\n445\t    const elapsedMs = Math.max(0, (now || 0) - createdAt);\n446\t    const node = card.querySelector('.pending-elapsed');\n447\t    if (node) node.textContent = formatElapsed(elapsedMs) + ' elapsed';\n448\t    return elapsedMs;\n449\t}\n450\t\n451\t/**\n452\t * Apply (or remove) the aged-pending treatment based on elapsed ms vs the 30s\n453\t * threshold. Pure client-side — survives a hung server (no SSE dependency).\n454\t * Returns true if the card is currently flagged aged.\n455\t */\n456\tfunction applyAgedTreatment(card, now) {\n457\t    const elapsedMs = tickPendingElapsed(card, now);\n458\t    const aged = elapsedMs >= PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS;\n459\t    card.classList.toggle('aged', aged);\n460\t    const note = card.querySelector('.pending-aged-note');\n461\t    if (note) note.style.display = aged ? '' : 'none';\n462\t    return aged;\n463\t}\n464\t\n465\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n466\t// (4) Error / clarification family — Degraded-State UX, filled client-side\n467\t//     from SSE error events\n468\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n469\t\n470\t// Display metadata per error variant. title + icon + a one-line description\n471\t// template. Variants accept both snake_case and kebab-case keys (normalized in\n472\t// _errorVariant()). Maps directly to the Degraded-State UX matrix rows.\n473\tvar ERROR_VARIANTS = {\n474\t    router_unavailable: {\n475\t        title: 'Router unavailable',\n476\t        icon: '🔌',\n477\t        detail: 'The LLM proxy (ZAI) was unreachable at the router stage, so this utterance could not be routed.'\n478\t    },\n479\t    all_sources_failed: {\n480\t        title: 'No data available',\n481\t        icon: '📭',\n482\t        detail: 'Every fetch source failed or timed out, so there is nothing to synthesize.'\n483\t    },\n484\t    synthesis_failed: {\n485\t        title: 'Summary unavailable',\n486\t        icon: '⚠️',\n487\t        detail: 'The raw data was fetched, but the summary could not be produced.'\n488\t    },\n489\t    malformed_router_output: {\n490\t        title: \"Couldn't parse that into intents\",\n491\t        icon: '🧩',\n492\t        detail: 'The router returned output the system could not interpret.'\n493\t    },\n494\t    no_match: {\n495\t        title: 'No matching project',\n496\t        icon: '❓',\n497\t        detail: 'No registered project matched this request.'\n498\t    }\n499\t};\n500\t\n501\tfunction _normalizeVariant(errorType) {\n502\t    if (!errorType) return 'no_match';\n503\t    const key = String(errorType).trim().replace(/-/g, '_').toLowerCase();\n504\t    return ERROR_VARIANTS[key] ? key : 'no_match';\n505\t}\n506\t\n507\t/**\n508\t * Build an error/clarification card from an SSE error event payload.\n509\t *\n510\t * @param {ErrorCardData} data - Error card data structure\n511\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .error-card element (data-builtin=\"error\")\n512\t *\n513\t * Per-source failure lists, the echoed utterance, raw degraded data, and any\n514\t * detail text are all inserted as text nodes (escaping contract).\n515\t */\n516\tfunction createErrorCard(data) {\n517\t    data = data || {};\n518\t    const variant = _normalizeVariant(data.error_type);\n519\t    const meta = ERROR_VARIANTS[variant];\n520\t\n521\t    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card error-card error-' + variant);\n522\t    card.dataset.builtin = 'error';\n523\t    card.dataset.errorType = variant;\n524\t    if (data.intent_id) card.dataset.intentId = String(data.intent_id);\n525\t    if (data.pending_id) card.dataset.pendingId = String(data.pending_id);\n526\t\n527\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n528\t        el('span', 'builtin-icon', [meta.icon]),\n529\t        el('span', 'builtin-title', [meta.title])\n530\t    ]));\n531\t\n532\t    const detail = data.detail || meta.detail;\n533\t    card.appendChild(el('p', 'error-detail', [detail]));\n534\t\n535\t    // Echo the utterance so nothing is ever lost (router_unavailable /\n536\t    // malformed_router_output rows). Rendered as text, never raw HTML.\n537\t    if (data.utterance) {\n538\t        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-utterance-wrap', [\n539\t            el('span', 'error-utterance-label', ['You said: ']),\n540\t            el('span', 'error-utterance', ['“' + data.utterance + '”'])\n541\t        ]));\n542\t    }\n543\t\n544\t    // Per-source failure list (all_sources_failed row).\n545\t    const sources = data.sources;\n546\t    if (Array.isArray(sources) && sources.length) {\n547\t        const ul = el('ul', 'error-source-list');\n548\t        sources.forEach(function (s) {\n549\t            const name = (s && (s.name || s.source)) || 'source';\n550\t            const reason = (s && (s.reason || s.error)) || 'failed';\n551\t            ul.appendChild(el('li', 'error-source', [\n552\t                el('span', 'error-source-name', [name + ': ']),\n553\t                el('span', 'error-source-reason', [reason])\n554\t            ]));\n555\t        });\n556\t        card.appendChild(ul);\n557\t    }\n558\t\n559\t    // Degraded raw data (synthesis_failed row): fetched data is never discarded.\n560\t    if (data.data != null) {\n561\t        const raw = typeof data.data === 'string' ? data.data : JSON.stringify(data.data, null, 2);\n562\t        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-raw-label', ['Fetched data']));\n563\t        card.appendChild(el('pre', 'error-raw', [raw]));\n564\t    }\n565\t\n566\t    // No-match clarification: list the registered projects so the user can pick.\n567\t    if (variant === 'no_match' && Array.isArray(data.registered_projects) && data.registered_projects.length) {\n568\t        const ul = el('ul', 'error-project-list');\n569\t        data.registered_projects.forEach(function (p) {\n570\t            const slug = typeof p === 'string' ? p : (p.slug || p.name || 'project');\n571\t            ul.appendChild(el('li', 'error-project', [slug]));\n572\t        });\n573\t        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-projects-wrap', [\n574\t            el('span', 'error-projects-label', ['Registered projects: ']),\n575\t            ul\n576\t        ]));\n577\t    }\n578\t\n579\t    // Recovery action button. Variant-aware label.\n580\t    const retryLabel = (variant === 'malformed_router_output' || variant === 'no_match')\n581\t        ? 'Edit & resend'\n582\t        : 'Retry';\n583\t    card.appendChild(el('button', 'error-retry', [retryLabel]));\n584\t\n585\t    return card;\n586\t}\n587\t\n588\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n589\t// Canvas Card Type Definitions\n590\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n591\t\n592\t/**\n593\t * @typedef {Object} StuckCardData\n594\t * @property {string} bead_id - } catch (e) { return [['value', data]]; }\n821\t    }\n822\t    if (Array.isArray(obj)) {\n823\t        return obj.slice(0, 50).map(function (v, i) { return [String(i), _stringify(v)]; });\n824\t    }\n825\t    if (typeof obj === 'object') {\n826\t        return Object.keys(obj).slice(0, 50).map(function (k) { return [k, _stringify(obj[k])]; });\n827\t    }\n828\t    return [['value', String(obj)]];\n829\t}\n830\t\n831\tfunction _stringify(v) {\n832\t    if (v == null) return '';\n833\t    if (typeof v === 'string') return v;\n834\t    if (typeof v === 'number' || typeof v === 'boolean') return String(v);\n835\t    try { return JSON.stringify(v); } catch (e) { return String(v); }\n836\t}\n837\t\n838\t// Export shim: browser globals are already in place (these are top-level\n839\t// function declarations). * ADC Canvas — rendering helpers.\n *\n * Pure functions that turn a /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics card dict into a DOM\n * topic-card element, and that build the four BUILT-IN card families the plan\n * ships as fixed templates in this served frontend (Component Library →\n * \"Built-in cards\"): (1) generic fallback, (2) first-run welcome card, (3)\n * pending/ack cards (submit-time placeholder, per-thread pending with\n * per-source progress, aged-pending treatment), and (4) the error/clarification\n * family from Degraded-State UX. They are kept in this separate, loadable\n * module (rather than inline in index.html) so the render contract can be\n * verified headlessly — tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js and\n * tests/e2e/canvas_builtin_runner.js load this exact file under a minimal DOM\n * shim and assert the rendered HTML, with no browser or network required.\n *\n * ESCAPING CONTRACT (the render-path escaping contract, see plan UI-Regen Agent\n * \"Escaping contract\"): every dynamic value — registry descriptions, utterance\n * text, per-source names, error detail, elapsed strings, LLM/worker-authored\n * free text — is interpolated through escapeHtml(), which sets it via a text\n * node (textContent → the browser's own escaper). No dynamic value is ever\n * spliced raw into innerHTML. The built-in builders below all go through the\n * same escapeHtml() the topic card uses, so they are bound by the identical\n * escaping contract.\n *\n * In the browser, index.html loads this via <script src=\"/canvas.js\"> before\n * the inline app script, so these declarations are globals just like they were\n * when they lived inline. In Node, the trailing export shim exposes them via\n * module.exports for the DOM tests.\n */\n\n// Node.js compatibility: ensure document is available in Node for headless tests\n// When loaded in Node (module.exports exists), make the global document accessible\n// to the el() helper function which uses document.createElement() and .createTextNode()\nif (typeof module !== 'undefined' && module.exports && typeof document === 'undefined') {\n    var document = global.document;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Tunables (exported so tests can assert against the same constants)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// Hot-path intents flag at 30s pending (their budget is 3s) — see plan, The\n// Async Path → Visible aging + Degraded-State UX aged-pending row. This flag is\n// applied PURELY client-side from the local placeholder's creation time so it\n// survives a hung/wedged server (no SSE dependency).\nvar PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS = 30000;\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Existing topic-card render helpers\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction formatStaleness(seconds) {\n    if (seconds < 60) return 'just now';\n    if (seconds < 3600) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 60)}m ago`;\n    if (seconds < 86400) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 3600)}h ago`;\n    return `${Math.floor(seconds / 86400)}d ago`;\n}\n\nfunction getStalenessLevel(seconds) {\n    if (seconds < 600) return 'fresh';      // 10 minutes\n    if (seconds < 3600) return 'stale';     // 1 hour\n    return 'very-stale';\n}\n\n/**\n * Build a topic card from topic data.\n *\n * @param {TopicCardData} cardData - Topic card data structure\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .topic-card element (or fallback-card element if card_fallback is set)\n */\nfunction createTopicCard(cardData) {\n    const topic = cardData.topic;\n    const staleness = cardData.staleness;\n    const latestResult = cardData.latest_result;\n\n    // When the result has card_fallback=True, render the built-in generic fallback card\n    // instead of the standard topic card. The fallback card is a complete card showing\n    // result.data as a key/value grid (plan: Component Library → \"Built-in generic\n    // fallback card\"). Topic metadata is preserved via data attributes for staleness\n    // tracking and querying, but visually the card is the fallback presentation.\n    if (latestResult && latestResult.card_fallback) {\n        const fallbackCard = createFallbackCard(latestResult);\n        // Preserve topic metadata for staleness tracking and querying\n        fallbackCard.dataset.topicId = topic.id;\n        fallbackCard.dataset.topicType = topic.type || 'adhoc';\n        fallbackCard.dataset.cardFallback = '1';\n        return fallbackCard;\n    }\n\n    const card = document.createElement('div');\n    card.className = 'topic-card';\n    card.dataset.topicId = topic.id;\n\n    const stalenessLevel = getStalenessLevel(staleness.seconds);\n    card.classList.add(stalenessLevel);\n\n    const typeClass = topic.type || 'adhoc';\n\n    // Expose the topic type as a data attribute alongside data-topic-id so cards\n    // are queryable by type with a robust, stable selector (e.g.\n    // [data-topic-type=\"research\"]) rather than relying on class names. The DOM\n    // verification suites assert both dataset attributes are present per card.\n    card.dataset.topicType = typeClass;\n\n    let html = `\n        <div class=\"topic-header\">\n            <div class=\"topic-label\">${escapeHtml(topic.label)}</div>\n            <div>\n                <span class=\"topic-type ${typeClass}\">${escapeHtml(topic.type || 'adhoc')}</span>\n                ${stalenessLevel !== 'fresh' ? `<span class=\"stale-badge ${stalenessLevel}\">STALE</span>` : ''}\n            </div>\n        </div>\n    `;\n\n    // Add latest result if available\n    if (latestResult) {\n        const urgencyClass = latestResult.urgency || 'normal';\n        html += `\n            <div class=\"result-content\">\n                <div class=\"result-summary\">\n                    ${escapeHtml(latestResult.summary)}\n                    <span class=\"urgency-badge ${urgencyClass}\">${escapeHtml(urgencyClass)}</span>\n                </div>\n        `;\n\n        if (latestResult.data) {\n            html += `<div class=\"result-data\">${escapeHtml(JSON.stringify(latestResult.data, null, 2))}</div>`;\n        }\n\n        html += `</div>`;\n    }\n\n    // Add staleness indicator\n    const timeAgo = formatStaleness(staleness.seconds);\n    html += `\n        <div class=\"staleness-indicator ${stalenessLevel}\">\n            <span class=\"staleness-dot ${stalenessLevel}\"></span>\n            <span>Updated ${timeAgo}</span>\n        </div>\n    `;\n\n    card.innerHTML = html;\n    return card;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Shared escaping + small DOM helpers (text-node based — escaping contract)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction escapeHtml(text) {\n    const div = document.createElement('div');\n    div.textContent = text == null ? '' : String(text);\n    return div.innerHTML;\n}\n\n/**\n * Build a DOM element with tag + className + a list of children/strings.\n *\n * Strings are appended as TEXT NODES (never spliced into innerHTML), which is\n * the escaping contract's literal form — a markup-looking log line renders as\n * literal text instead of breaking layout. Use this for any value that must be\n * inserted verbatim as text.\n */\nfunction el(tag, className, children) {\n    const node = document.createElement(tag);\n    if (className) node.className = className;\n    (children || []).forEach(function (c) {\n        if (c == null) return;\n        if (typeof c === 'string' || typeof c === 'number') {\n            node.appendChild(document.createTextNode(String(c)));\n        } else {\n            node.appendChild(c);\n        }\n    });\n    return node;\n}\n\n// Seconds → \"0s\" / \"12s\" / \"1m 05s\" elapsed label for pending cards.\nfunction formatElapsed(ms) {\n    const s = Math.max(0, Math.floor((ms || 0) / 1000));\n    if (s < 60) return s + 's';\n    const m = Math.floor(s / 60);\n    const rem = s % 60;\n    return m + 'm ' + (rem < 10 ? '0' : '') + rem + 's';\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// (2) First-run welcome card — built-in, zero DB dependence\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// Default example utterances used when the registry yields no project with a\n// supported intent to derive one from. These never touch the component DB.\nvar DEFAULT_EXAMPLE_UTTERANCES = [\n    'Has the options pipeline caught up?',\n    'What is the state of the ibkr mcp?',\n    'Queue up a research task on recent pipeline errors — no rush.'\n];\n\n// Map a supported intent type to an example utterance fragment, so the welcome\n// card's examples are drawn from the projects the router actually knows about\n// (plan: Cold start & demo seed — \"2-3 example utterances drawn from those\n// projects' supported intents\").\nvar INTENT_EXAMPLES = {\n    status: 'What is the status of {proj}?',\n    action: 'Restart the {proj} deployment.',\n    brainstorm: 'Brainstorm trade-offs for {proj}.',\n    lookup: 'Pull up recent logs for {proj}.',\n    'task-profile': 'Queue up a research task on {proj} — no rush.',\n    'self-modification': 'Add an alias to the {proj} registry entry.',\n    'monitoring-config': 'Watch {proj} for pod restarts.',\n    reminder: 'Remind me to check {proj} in an hour.'\n};\n\nfunction _deriveExamples(projects) {\n    const out = [];\n    const seen = {};\n    // Prefer projects that carry a description (registry.yaml entries) so the\n    // welcome examples reference real, described projects — not bare discovered\n    // repos with auto-README blurbs.\n    const ordered = (projects || []).slice().sort(function (a, b) {\n        const ad = a && a.description ? 0 : 1;\n        const bd = b && b.description ? 0 : 1;\n        return ad - bd;\n    });\n    ordered.forEach(function (p) {\n        if (out.length >= 3) return;\n        (p.intent_support || []).forEach(function (intent) {\n            if (out.length >= 3 || seen[intent]) return;\n            const tmpl = INTENT_EXAMPLES[intent];\n            if (!tmpl) return;\n            seen[intent] = true;\n            out.push(tmpl.replace('{proj}', p.slug || p.name || 'the project'));\n        });\n    });\n    return out.length ? out : DEFAULT_EXAMPLE_UTTERANCES.slice(0, 3);\n}\n\n// Cap the welcome-card project list so a 38-repo registry doesn't overwhelm the\n// first frame. The first N are shown; an \"+N more\" note counts the rest.\nvar WELCOME_PROJECT_CAP = 12;\n\n/**\n * Build the first-run welcome card.\n *\n * @param {Array} projects  registry entries [{slug,name,description,intent_support,aliases}]\n * @param {string} [description]  one-line description of aide-de-camp\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .welcome-card element (data-builtin=\"welcome\")\n *\n * Renders even against an empty component library / DB — the project list comes\n * from config/registry.yaml (served at /api/v1/registry), not the DB. The first\n * real result replaces it (the canvas drops it once any topic card exists).\n */\nfunction createWelcomeCard(projects, description) {\n    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card welcome-card');\n    card.dataset.builtin = 'welcome';\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n        el('span', 'builtin-icon', ['👋']),\n        el('span', 'builtin-title', ['Welcome to ADC'])\n    ]));\n\n    card.appendChild(el('p', 'builtin-desc', [\n        description || 'A single input surface that routes what you say to the right project, fetches in parallel, and renders results as live cards.'\n    ]));\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-section-title', ['Registered projects']));\n    const list = el('ul', 'builtin-project-list');\n    (projects || []).forEach(function (p) {\n        const slug = p.slug || p.name || 'project';\n        const desc = p.description || '';\n        const intents = (p.intent_support || []).join(' · ');\n        const li = el('li', 'builtin-project');\n        li.appendChild(el('strong', 'builtin-project-slug', [slug]));\n        if (desc) li.appendChild(document.createTextNode(' — ' + desc));\n        if (intents) li.appendChild(el('span', 'builtin-intents', [' ' + intents]));\n        list.appendChild(li);\n    });\n    if (!(projects && projects.length)) {\n        list.appendChild(el('li', 'builtin-project', ['No projects registered yet.']));\n    }\n    card.appendChild(list);\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-section-title', ['Try asking']));\n    const examples = el('ul', 'builtin-examples');\n    _deriveExamples(projects).forEach(function (ex) {\n        examples.appendChild(el('li', 'builtin-example', [ex]));\n    });\n    card.appendChild(examples);\n\n    return card;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Container decision — the headlessly-testable core of loadTopics()\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Decide what the canvas topic-container shows for a given card set.\n *\n * This is the shared render path index.html's ``loadTopics()`` calls after\n * ``GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics``: on a fresh session with zero cards it\n * shows the first-run welcome card (built from the registry project list — no\n * DB/result dependence); the moment any real card exists it shows only topic\n * cards, which drops any welcome card (never alongside real cards). The caller\n * clears the container and appends the returned nodes.\n *\n * Kept here (not inline in index.html) so the zero→welcome / first-result→drop\n * contract is verifiable headlessly: ``tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js`` drives\n * this exact function (``--container`` mode) and asserts the welcome card\n * renders from an empty card set and is absent once a real card is present.\n *\n * @param {Array} [cards]      card dicts from /topics (empty → welcome card)\n * @param {Array} [projects]   registry entries for the welcome card\n * @param {string} [description] one-line description for the welcome card\n * @returns {HTMLElement[]}    child nodes for the container\n */\nfunction buildContainerChildren(cards, projects, description) {\n    if (!cards || cards.length === 0) {\n        return [createWelcomeCard(projects, description)];\n    }\n    return cards.map(function (c) { return createTopicCard(c); });\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// (3) Pending/ack cards — submit-time placeholder + per-thread pending\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Build a submit-time pending placeholder.\n *\n * Created LOCALLY at dispatch() submit time, BEFORE any server response, so a\n * hung/wedged server still leaves a card on canvas to age (plan, Escalate\n * Strand → Pending/ack card render path). It splits into per-thread pending\n * cards when the dispatch ack (the /dispatch HTTP response) carries intent_ids.\n *\n * @param {string} utterance   the raw text being dispatched\n * @param {number} createdAt   Date.now() at placeholder creation (ms epoch)\n * @param {string} [pendingId] stable id for this placeholder (utterance id)\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .pending-card.placeholder (data-builtin=\"pending\")\n */\nfunction createPendingPlaceholderCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId) {\n    const card = _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId, 'Sending…');\n    card.classList.add('placeholder');\n    card.dataset.pendingKind = 'placeholder';\n    return card;\n}\n\n/**\n * Build a per-thread pending card (one per dispatch-ack intent_id). Carries the\n * per-source progress state ('3/5 sources in') and the elapsed-time footer.\n *\n * @param {string} intentId    the intent this thread tracks (result_created key)\n * @param {string} utterance   the originating utterance fragment/text\n * @param {number} createdAt   ms epoch inherited from the placeholder\n * @param {object} [progress]  {completed, total} initial per-source progress\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .pending-card.thread (data-builtin=\"pending\")\n */\nfunction createPendingThreadCard(intentId, utterance, createdAt, progress) {\n    const card = _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, intentId, 'Working on it…');\n    card.classList.add('thread');\n    card.dataset.pendingKind = 'thread';\n    if (progress) _setProgress(card, progress);\n    return card;\n}\n\n/**\n * Split a submit-time placeholder into per-thread pending cards on the dispatch\n * ack. The /dispatch ack response carries ``intent_ids`` — one per routed\n * thread — so this produces one per-thread pending card per intent_id, each\n * inheriting the placeholder's ``createdAt`` (so the 30s aged timer is\n * continuous across the split — the timer started at submit, not at ack) and\n * the originating utterance. (plan, Escalate Strand → Pending/ack card render\n * path: \"splits into per-thread pending cards when the dispatch ack arrives\".)\n *\n * This is the headlessly-testable core of the split: index.html's dispatch()\n * calls it with the ack's intent_ids and replaces the placeholder DOM node with\n * the returned cards; the Node DOM shim exercises it directly (no browser) to\n * prove the split is one-card-per-thread.\n *\n * @param {string} utterance   the originating utterance text\n * @param {number} createdAt   ms epoch inherited from the placeholder\n * @param {string[]} intentIds the ack's intent_ids (one per routed thread)\n * @returns {HTMLElement[]} one .pending-card.thread per intent_id\n */\nfunction splitPlaceholderToThreads(utterance, createdAt, intentIds) {\n    return (intentIds || []).map(function (id) {\n        return createPendingThreadCard(id, utterance, createdAt);\n    });\n}\n\n// Shared skeleton for both placeholder + per-thread pending cards.\nfunction _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId, titleText) {\n    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card pending-card');\n    card.dataset.builtin = 'pending';\n    card.dataset.createdAt = String(createdAt || 0);\n    if (pendingId) card.dataset.pendingId = String(pendingId);\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n        el('span', 'pending-spinner'),\n        el('span', 'builtin-title pending-title', [titleText || 'Pending…'])\n    ]));\n\n    if (utterance) {\n        card.appendChild(el('p', 'pending-utterance', [utterance]));\n    }\n\n    const progress = el('div', 'pending-progress');\n    progress.style.display = 'none';\n    card.appendChild(progress);\n\n    const elapsed = el('div', 'pending-elapsed', [\n        formatElapsed(0) + ' elapsed'\n    ]);\n    card.appendChild(elapsed);\n\n    // Aged note (hidden until the 30s threshold) — see applyAgedTreatment().\n    const agedNote = el('div', 'pending-aged-note');\n    agedNote.style.display = 'none';\n    agedNote.appendChild(document.createTextNode('Taking longer than expected — the server may be hung. '));\n    const retry = el('button', 'pending-retry', ['Retry']);\n    agedNote.appendChild(retry);\n    card.appendChild(agedNote);\n\n    return card;\n}\n\nfunction _setProgress(card, progress) {\n    const node = card.querySelector('.pending-progress');\n    if (!node || !progress) return;\n    const completed = Math.max(0, parseInt(progress.completed, 10) || 0);\n    const total = Math.max(0, parseInt(progress.total, 10) || 0);\n    node.textContent = completed + '/' + total + ' sources in';\n    node.dataset.completed = String(completed);\n    node.dataset.total = String(total);\n    node.style.display = total > 0 ? '' : 'none';\n}\n\n/**\n * Recompute the elapsed-time footer for a pending card against `now` (ms).\n * Returns the elapsed ms. Pure: no timers, no globals beyond the card's own\n * data-createdAt — so the headless harness can drive it via a mock clock.\n */\nfunction tickPendingElapsed(card, now) {\n    const createdAt = parseInt(card.dataset.createdAt, 10) || 0;\n    const elapsedMs = Math.max(0, (now || 0) - createdAt);\n    const node = card.querySelector('.pending-elapsed');\n    if (node) node.textContent = formatElapsed(elapsedMs) + ' elapsed';\n    return elapsedMs;\n}\n\n/**\n * Apply (or remove) the aged-pending treatment based on elapsed ms vs the 30s\n * threshold. Pure client-side — survives a hung server (no SSE dependency).\n * Returns true if the card is currently flagged aged.\n */\nfunction applyAgedTreatment(card, now) {\n    const elapsedMs = tickPendingElapsed(card, now);\n    const aged = elapsedMs >= PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS;\n    card.classList.toggle('aged', aged);\n    const note = card.querySelector('.pending-aged-note');\n    if (note) note.style.display = aged ? '' : 'none';\n    return aged;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// (4) Error / clarification family — Degraded-State UX, filled client-side\n//     from SSE error events\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// Display metadata per error variant. title + icon + a one-line description\n// template. Variants accept both snake_case and kebab-case keys (normalized in\n// _errorVariant()). Maps directly to the Degraded-State UX matrix rows.\nvar ERROR_VARIANTS = {\n    router_unavailable: {\n        title: 'Router unavailable',\n        icon: '🔌',\n        detail: 'The LLM proxy (ZAI) was unreachable at the router stage, so this utterance could not be routed.'\n    },\n    all_sources_failed: {\n        title: 'No data available',\n        icon: '📭',\n        detail: 'Every fetch source failed or timed out, so there is nothing to synthesize.'\n    },\n    synthesis_failed: {\n        title: 'Summary unavailable',\n        icon: '⚠️',\n        detail: 'The raw data was fetched, but the summary could not be produced.'\n    },\n    malformed_router_output: {\n        title: \"Couldn't parse that into intents\",\n        icon: '🧩',\n        detail: 'The router returned output the system could not interpret.'\n    },\n    no_match: {\n        title: 'No matching project',\n        icon: '❓',\n        detail: 'No registered project matched this request.'\n    }\n};\n\nfunction _normalizeVariant(errorType) {\n    if (!errorType) return 'no_match';\n    const key = String(errorType).trim().replace(/-/g, '_').toLowerCase();\n    return ERROR_VARIANTS[key] ? key : 'no_match';\n}\n\n/**\n * Build an error/clarification card from an SSE error event payload.\n *\n * @param {ErrorCardData} data - Error card data structure\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .error-card element (data-builtin=\"error\")\n *\n * Per-source failure lists, the echoed utterance, raw degraded data, and any\n * detail text are all inserted as text nodes (escaping contract).\n */\nfunction createErrorCard(data) {\n    data = data || {};\n    const variant = _normalizeVariant(data.error_type);\n    const meta = ERROR_VARIANTS[variant];\n\n    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card error-card error-' + variant);\n    card.dataset.builtin = 'error';\n    card.dataset.errorType = variant;\n    if (data.intent_id) card.dataset.intentId = String(data.intent_id);\n    if (data.pending_id) card.dataset.pendingId = String(data.pending_id);\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n        el('span', 'builtin-icon', [meta.icon]),\n        el('span', 'builtin-title', [meta.title])\n    ]));\n\n    const detail = data.detail || meta.detail;\n    card.appendChild(el('p', 'error-detail', [detail]));\n\n    // Echo the utterance so nothing is ever lost (router_unavailable /\n    // malformed_router_output rows). Rendered as text, never raw HTML.\n    if (data.utterance) {\n        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-utterance-wrap', [\n            el('span', 'error-utterance-label', ['You said: ']),\n            el('span', 'error-utterance', ['“' + data.utterance + '”'])\n        ]));\n    }\n\n    // Per-source failure list (all_sources_failed row).\n    const sources = data.sources;\n    if (Array.isArray(sources) && sources.length) {\n        const ul = el('ul', 'error-source-list');\n        sources.forEach(function (s) {\n            const name = (s && (s.name || s.source)) || 'source';\n            const reason = (s && (s.reason || s.error)) || 'failed';\n            ul.appendChild(el('li', 'error-source', [\n                el('span', 'error-source-name', [name + ': ']),\n                el('span', 'error-source-reason', [reason])\n            ]));\n        });\n        card.appendChild(ul);\n    }\n\n    // Degraded raw data (synthesis_failed row): fetched data is never discarded.\n    if (data.data != null) {\n        const raw = typeof data.data === 'string' ? data.data : JSON.stringify(data.data, null, 2);\n        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-raw-label', ['Fetched data']));\n        card.appendChild(el('pre', 'error-raw', [raw]));\n    }\n\n    // No-match clarification: list the registered projects so the user can pick.\n    if (variant === 'no_match' && Array.isArray(data.registered_projects) && data.registered_projects.length) {\n        const ul = el('ul', 'error-project-list');\n        data.registered_projects.forEach(function (p) {\n            const slug = typeof p === 'string' ? p : (p.slug || p.name || 'project');\n            ul.appendChild(el('li', 'error-project', [slug]));\n        });\n        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-projects-wrap', [\n            el('span', 'error-projects-label', ['Registered projects: ']),\n            ul\n        ]));\n    }\n\n    // Recovery action button. Variant-aware label.\n    const retryLabel = (variant === 'malformed_router_output' || variant === 'no_match')\n        ? 'Edit & resend'\n        : 'Retry';\n    card.appendChild(el('button', 'error-retry', [retryLabel]));\n\n    return card;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Canvas Card Type Definitions\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * @typedef {Object} StuckCardData\n * @property {string} bead_id - The bead reference ID that is stuck\n * @property {string} stuck_reason - The reason why the bead is stuck (refusal reason)\n * @property {number} [refusal_count] - Number of refusals that caused the stuck state\n * @property {string} [message] - Optional message describing the stuck state\n * @property {string} [action_hint] - Optional hint for user action to resolve\n */\n\n/**\n * @typedef {Object} FailedCardData\n * @property {string} bead_id - The bead reference ID that failed\n * @property {string} failure_reason - The reason why the task failed\n * @property {string} [error_type] - Optional error type/category\n * @property {string} [message] - Optional message describing the failure\n */\n\n/**\n * @typedef {Object} ErrorCardData\n * @property {string} error_type - The error variant type\n * @property {string} [utterance] - The original user utterance\n * @property {string} [detail] - Error detail message\n * @property {Array<{name?: string, source?: string, reason?: string, error?: string}>} [sources] - Failed sources list\n * @property {*} [data] - Raw degraded data\n * @property {string} [intent_id] - Associated intent ID\n * @property {string} [pending_id] - Associated pending ID\n * @property {Array<string|{slug?: string, name?: string}>} [registered_projects] - Projects for no-match variant\n */\n\n/**\n * @typedef {Object} PendingCardData\n * @property {string} utterance - The utterance being processed\n * @property {number} createdAt - Creation timestamp (ms epoch)\n * @property {string} [pendingId] - Stable ID for this placeholder\n * @property {number} [completed] - Number of completed sources (for progress)\n * @property {number} [total] - Total number of sources (for progress)\n */\n\n/**\n * @typedef {Object} TopicCardData\n * @property {Object} topic - The topic object\n * @property {string} topic.id - Topic ID\n * @property {string} topic.label - Topic label\n * @property {string} [topic.type] - Topic type (project, research, personal, exception, compound)\n * @property {Object} staleness - Staleness info\n * @property {number} staleness.seconds - Seconds since last update\n * @property {Object} [latest_result] - Optional latest result\n * @property {string} latest_result.summary - Result summary\n * @property {string} [latest_result.urgency] - Result urgency (critical, high, normal, low)\n * @property {*} [latest_result.data] - Result data\n * @property {boolean} [latest_result.card_fallback] - True when no component matched (fallback card rendered)\n */\n\n/**\n * @typedef {Object} FallbackCardData\n * @property {string} [summary] - Result summary\n * @property {*} [data] - Result data\n * @property {string} [urgency] - Result urgency\n */\n\n/**\n * @typedef {StuckCardData | FailedCardData | ErrorCardData | PendingCardData | TopicCardData | FallbackCardData} CardData\n * Union type for all card data structures\n */\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// (5) Stuck/Failed cards — circuit breaker and terminal failure cards\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Build a stuck card for a fenced bead.\n *\n * Plan §10 The Async Path: displayed when a bead is fenced due to repeated\n * refusals or timeout. Shows the bead reference, refusal count, and latest reason.\n *\n * @param {StuckCardData} data - Stuck card data structure\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .stuck-card element (data-builtin=\"stuck\")\n */\nfunction createStuckCard(data) {\n    data = data || {};\n    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card stuck-card');\n    card.dataset.builtin = 'stuck';\n    if (data.bead_id) card.dataset.beadId = String(data.bead_id);\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n        el('span', 'builtin-icon', ['🚧']),\n        el('span', 'builtin-title', ['Task stuck — needs your input'])\n    ]));\n\n    if (data.message) {\n        card.appendChild(el('p', 'stuck-message', [data.message]));\n    }\n\n    if (data.stuck_reason) {\n        card.appendChild(el('div', 'stuck-reason-wrap', [\n            el('span', 'stuck-reason-label', ['Reason: ']),\n            el('span', 'stuck-reason', [data.stuck_reason])\n        ]));\n    }\n\n    const meta = el('div', 'stuck-meta');\n    let metaChildCount = 0;\n    if (data.refusal_count != null) {\n        meta.appendChild(el('span', 'stuck-refusal-count', [\n            'Refusals: ' + String(data.refusal_count)\n        ]));\n        metaChildCount++;\n    }\n    if (data.bead_id) {\n        if (metaChildCount > 0) {\n            meta.appendChild(document.createTextNode(' • '));\n        }\n        meta.appendChild(el('span', 'stuck-bead-id', ['Bead: ' + data.bead_id]));\n        metaChildCount++;\n    }\n    if (metaChildCount > 0) {\n        card.appendChild(meta);\n    }\n\n    if (data.action_hint) {\n        card.appendChild(el('p', 'stuck-action-hint', [data.action_hint]));\n    }\n\n    // View bead button\n    const viewBtn = el('button', 'stuck-view-bead', ['View bead']);\n    card.appendChild(viewBtn);\n\n    return card;\n}\n\n/**\n * Build a failed card for a terminal failure.\n *\n * Plan §10 The Async Path: displayed when an intent fails non-recoverably\n * (worker crash, invalid input, etc.). Shows failure reason and context.\n *\n * @param {FailedCardData} data - Failed card data structure\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .failed-card element (data-builtin=\"failed\")\n */\nfunction createFailedCard(data) {\n    data = data || {};\n    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card failed-card');\n    card.dataset.builtin = 'failed';\n    if (data.bead_id) card.dataset.beadId = String(data.bead_id);\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n        el('span', 'builtin-icon', ['❌']),\n        el('span', 'builtin-title', ['Task failed'])\n    ]));\n\n    if (data.message) {\n        card.appendChild(el('p', 'failed-message', [data.message]));\n    }\n\n    if (data.failure_reason) {\n        card.appendChild(el('div', 'failed-reason-wrap', [\n            el('span', 'failed-reason-label', ['Reason: ']),\n            el('span', 'failed-reason', [data.failure_reason])\n        ]));\n    }\n\n    if (data.error_type) {\n        card.appendChild(el('div', 'failed-error-type', [\n            'Error type: ' + data.error_type\n        ]));\n    }\n\n    if (data.bead_id) {\n        card.appendChild(el('div', 'failed-bead-id', ['Bead: ' + data.bead_id]));\n    }\n\n    // Retry button\n    const retryBtn = el('button', 'failed-retry', ['Retry']);\n    card.appendChild(retryBtn);\n\n    return card;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// (1) Generic fallback card — key/value grid over result.data + summary\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Build the generic fallback card for a result that matched no component\n * (plan, Component Library → \"Built-in generic fallback card\"). Key/value grid\n * over result.data plus the summary line — every value escaped per the\n * rendering escaping contract.\n *\n * @param {FallbackCardData} result - Fallback card data structure\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .fallback-card element (data-builtin=\"fallback\")\n */\nfunction createFallbackCard(result) {\n    result = result || {};\n    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card fallback-card');\n    card.dataset.builtin = 'fallback';\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n        el('span', 'builtin-icon', ['🗒️']),\n        el('span', 'builtin-title', ['Result'])\n    ]));\n\n    if (result.summary) {\n        card.appendChild(el('p', 'fallback-summary', [result.summary]));\n    }\n\n    const data = result.data;\n    const rows = _fallbackRows(data);\n    if (rows.length) {\n        const grid = el('div', 'fallback-grid');\n        rows.forEach(function (r) {\n            grid.appendChild(el('div', 'fallback-key', [r[0]]));\n            grid.appendChild(el('div', 'fallback-val', [r[1]]));\n        });\n        card.appendChild(grid);\n    }\n\n    if (result.urgency) {\n        card.appendChild(el('span', 'urgency-badge ' + result.urgency, [result.urgency]));\n    }\n\n    return card;\n}\n\n// Flatten result.data into [key, value] string pairs for the fallback grid.\nfunction _fallbackRows(data) {\n    if (data == null) return [];\n    let obj = data;\n    if (typeof data === 'string') {\n        try { obj = JSON.parse(data); } catch (e) { return [['value', data]]; }\n    }\n    if (Array.isArray(obj)) {\n        return obj.slice(0, 50).map(function (v, i) { return [String(i), _stringify(v)]; });\n    }\n    if (typeof obj === 'object') {\n        return Object.keys(obj).slice(0, 50).map(function (k) { return [k, _stringify(obj[k])]; });\n    }\n    return [['value', String(obj)]];\n}\n\nfunction _stringify(v) {\n    if (v == null) return '';\n    if (typeof v === 'string') return v;\n    if (typeof v === 'number' || typeof v === 'boolean') return String(v);\n    try { return JSON.stringify(v); } catch (e) { return String(v); }\n}\n\n// Export shim: browser globals are already in place (these are top-level\n// function declarations). 'PendingCardData',\n            TopicCardData: 'TopicCardData',\n            FallbackCardData: 'FallbackCardData',\n            CardData: 'CardData'  // Union type\n        }\n    };\n}\n\n// Also Iterate over result.data key/value pairs\n        # 2. Escape each value using escapeHtml()\n        # 3. Render escaped values as text nodes (not raw HTML)\n\n        # Verify escaping contract:\n        # - '<' becomes '&lt;'\n        # - '>' becomes '&gt;'\n        # - '\"' becomes '&quot;'\n        # - '&' becomes '&amp;'\n\n        escaped_log = result_data[\"log_line\"].replace('<', '&lt;').replace('>', '&gt;')\n        assert '&lt;script&gt;' in escaped_log\n        assert '<script>' not in escaped_log\n\n    def test_fallback_card_preserves_safe_text(self):\n        \"\"\"TC-FB-010: Fallback card should preserve safe text as-is.\"\"\"\n        result_data = {\n            \"message\": \"All systems operational\",\n            \"count\": 42,\n        }\n\n        # Safe text should be rendered as-is (no escaping needed)\n        assert result_data[\"message\"] == \"All systems operational\"\n        assert result_data[\"count\"] == 42\n\n\n# =============================================================================\n# Integration Tests - '' : String(text);\n    return div.innerHTML;\n}\n```\n- **All values** are HTML-escaped at render time\n- **Text nodes only**: `el()` helper inserts content as text nodes, never raw HTML\n- **Security**: Markup-like log lines render as literal text\n\n### 5.
